(Two house-shaped sarcophagi inside) |Artifacts=Numerous grave goods |Accompanying_mounds= |Construction_period=Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} (or early 6th century) |Buried_person= |Imperial_tomb= |Cultural_property=Included in the national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} "Oiwayama Kofun Group" |Tangible_cultural_property= |Special_notes= |Map=Japan Shiga |Icon=円墳 |Label=Maruyama Kofun |Label_position=bottom }} '''Maruyama Kofun''' (円山古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Yasu|ja|野洲市}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}, in the Koshinohara area. It has a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}} shape and is one of the burial mounds that comprise the {{ill|Ōiwayama Kofun Cluster|ja|大岩山古墳群}}, designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}}. == Overview == This kofun was constructed at the northern tip of a hill extending from Oiwayama to the right bank of the Yasu River in southern Shiga Prefecture. While the mound has been subject to looting, recent excavations were conducted as part of historic site maintenance efforts. The mound is circular, measuring 28 meters in diameter and approximately 8 meters in height {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}{{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. Initially thought to be a {{ill|Scallop-shaped burial mound|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}, it is now recognized as a round kofun {{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture Cultural Property Learning Sheet|2004}}. The burial facility consists of a one-sided {{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室}} opening westward {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Inside the chamber are two house-shaped sarcophagi: one made of Aso welded tuff (Aso pink stone/Makado stone) and another made of {{ill|Futagoyama|ja|二上山}} tuff. These sarcophagi, along with a variety of grave goods such as silver ear ornaments, iron weapons, and over 10,000 glass beads, were uncovered during excavations {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}{{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture Cultural Property Learning Sheet|2004}}. The construction period is estimated to be the early 6th century, placing it in the late Kofun period {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. It is believed to be the tomb of a regional leader and represents the first use of Aso welded tuff sarcophagi in the Yasu River basin {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. The burial goods reflect the power and influence of the entombed individual. The kofun site was designated a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} in 1941. It has since been preserved and opened to the public as part of the Sakuraba Historic Park, though access to the stone chamber is restricted and allowed only during annual autumn openings. == Archaeological History == * **1941**: Designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} (December 13) {{National Cultural Heritage Database|401|1579|Oiwayama Kofun Group}}. * **1982**: Surveyed {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. * **1985**: Six additional mounds were incorporated into the previously designated historic sites "Maruyama Kofun" and "Kozan Kofun," leading to the renaming of the designation as "Oiwayama Kofun Group" . * **2001**: Excavations and maintenance reports by the Yasu Town Board of Education. * **2001**: Sakuraba Historic Park opened on November 3 {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the burial chamber}}]] [[File:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the burial chamber}}]] The burial facility is a one-sided {{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the west {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The chamber floor is paved with pebbles {{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture Cultural Property Learning Sheet|2004}}. Inside the chamber are two sarcophagi: * **Hollowed-out house-shaped sarcophagus**: Located near the front of the chamber and presumed to be the primary burial. Made of Aso welded tuff (Aso pink stone/Makado stone), it measures 2.85 meters in length, 1.46 meters in width, and 1.83 meters in height. The lid stone has rope-loop projections and is painted with red pigment {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. * **Assembled house-shaped sarcophagus**: Positioned at the back of the chamber, made of Futagoyama tuff. Its lid and front stones are damaged, but the base stone measures 2.45 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width, while the sarcophagus body is about 0.8 meters in height. This is considered one of the oldest examples of Futagoyama tuff sarcophagi in Shiga Prefecture {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. This is considered one of the oldest examples of Futagoyama tuff sarcophagi in Shiga Prefecture {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Artifacts == Notable grave goods recovered include {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}: * **Ornaments** * Silver ear ornaments with pendants * Silver spiral earrings * Step pendants * Silver hollow beads * ~10,000 glass beads {{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture Cultural Property Learning Sheet|2004}} * **Weapons** * Lamellar armor * Helmet with horizontal riveting * Sword with twisted ring pommel * Silver spiral fittings * Small knife with antler handle * Over 10 iron spearheads * Over 500 iron arrowheads * Quiver * **Horse gear** * Saddle fittings * Stirrups * Bits == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Maruyama Kofun |image=[[File:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), entrance.jpg|280px]]
Mound and stone chamber entrance |Other_name= |Group={{ill|Oiwayama Kofun Group|ja|大岩山古墳群}} |location={{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}{{ill|Yasu|ja|野洲市}} Koshinohara (Azamaruyama)
(Sakuraba Historic Park) |Lat_deg=35|Lat_min=4|Lat_sec=26.82 |Lon_deg=136|Lon_min=2|Lon_sec=28.35 |ISO=JP-25 |Shape={{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}} |Scale=Diameter 28m
Height 8m |Burial_facility=One-sided {{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室}}
(Two house-shaped sarcophagi inside) |Artifacts=Numerous grave goods |Accompanying_mounds= |Construction_period=Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} (or early 6th century) |Buried_person= |Imperial_tomb= |Cultural_property=Included in the national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} "Oiwayama Kofun Group" |Tangible_cultural_property= |Special_notes= |Map=Japan Shiga |Icon=円墳 |Label=Maruyama Kofun |Label_position=bottom }} '''Maruyama Kofun''' (円山古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Yasu|ja|野洲市}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}, in the Koshinohara area. It has a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}} shape and is one of the burial mounds that comprise the {{ill|Ōiwayama Kofun Cluster|ja|大岩山古墳群}}, designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}}. == Overview == This kofun was constructed at the northern tip of a hill extending from Oiwayama to the right bank of the Yasu River in southern Shiga Prefecture. While the mound has been subject to looting, recent excavations were conducted as part of historic site maintenance efforts. The mound is circular, measuring 28 meters in diameter and approximately 8 meters in height {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}{{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. Initially thought to be a {{ill|Scallop-shaped burial mound|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}, it is now recognized as a round kofun {{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture Cultural Property Learning Sheet|2004}}. The burial facility consists of a one-sided {{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室}} opening westward {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Inside the chamber are two house-shaped sarcophagi: one made of Aso welded tuff (Aso pink stone/Makado stone) and another made of {{ill|Futagoyama|ja|二上山}} tuff. These sarcophagi, along with a variety of grave goods such as silver ear ornaments, iron weapons, and over 10,000 glass beads, were uncovered during excavations {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}{{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture Cultural Property Learning Sheet|2004}}. The construction period is estimated to be the early 6th century, placing it in the late Kofun period {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. It is believed to be the tomb of a regional leader and represents the first use of Aso welded tuff sarcophagi in the Yasu River basin {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. The burial goods reflect the power and influence of the entombed individual. The kofun site was designated a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} in 1941. It has since been preserved and opened to the public as part of the Sakuraba Historic Park, though access to the stone chamber is restricted and allowed only during annual autumn openings. == Archaeological History == * **1941**: Designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} (December 13) {{National Cultural Heritage Database|401|1579|Oiwayama Kofun Group}}. * **1982**: Surveyed {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. * **1985**: Six additional mounds were incorporated into the previously designated historic sites "Maruyama Kofun" and "Kozan Kofun," leading to the renaming of the designation as "Oiwayama Kofun Group" . * **2001**: Excavations and maintenance reports by the Yasu Town Board of Education. * **2001**: Sakuraba Historic Park opened on November 3 {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the burial chamber}}]] [[File:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the burial chamber}}]] The burial facility is a one-sided {{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the west {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Its dimensions are as follows {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}{{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}: * Total length: 10.8 meters * Chamber: Length 4.3 meters, width 2.4 meters, height 3.1 meters * Passage: Length 6.5 meters, width 1.4 meters The passage descends approximately 1 meter from the entrance to the chamber floor, with the ceiling stones also stepping down {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The chamber floor is paved with pebbles {{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture Cultural Property Learning Sheet|2004}}. Inside the chamber are two sarcophagi: * **Hollowed-out house-shaped sarcophagus**: Located near the front of the chamber and presumed to be the primary burial. Made of Aso welded tuff (Aso pink stone/Makado stone), it measures 2.85 meters in length, 1.46 meters in width, and 1.83 meters in height. The lid stone has rope-loop projections and is painted with red pigment {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. * **Assembled house-shaped sarcophagus**: Positioned at the back of the chamber, made of Futagoyama tuff. Its lid and front stones are damaged, but the base stone measures 2.45 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width, while the sarcophagus body is about 0.8 meters in height. This is considered one of the oldest examples of Futagoyama tuff sarcophagi in Shiga Prefecture {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), genshitsu-2.jpg|Chamber (facing rear wall) Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), sendou-2.jpg|Passage (facing entrance) Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), sendou-1.jpg|Passage (facing chamber) == Artifacts == Notable grave goods recovered include {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}: * **Ornaments** * Silver ear ornaments with pendants * Silver spiral earrings * Step pendants * Silver hollow beads * ~10,000 glass beads {{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture Cultural Property Learning Sheet|2004}} * **Weapons** * Lamellar armor * Helmet with horizontal riveting * Sword with twisted ring pommel * Silver spiral fittings * Small knife with antler handle * Over 10 iron spearheads * Over 500 iron arrowheads * Quiver * **Horse gear** * Saddle fittings * Stirrups * Bits == Related Facilities == * Sakuraba Historic Park Information Center (Yasu City, Koshinohara) * Yasu City Museum of History and Folklore (Dotaku Museum, Yasu City, Tsuji Town) == References == Sakuraba Historic Park Information Center, linked from Yasu City Tourism Association) {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. * Explanation boards (former Yasu Town Board of Education, 2001). * Prefectural publications: * {{PDFlink|[http://www.shiga-ec.ed.jp/www/contents/1438304524592/html/common/other/55d173d3025.pdf "Oiwayama Kofun Group"]}} Shiga Prefectural Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Section, 2004. * Encyclopedias: * {{Cite book|title=Maruyama Kofun|series=Continued Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Shuppan|year=2002}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Ōiwayama revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name=Maruyama Kofun |image=[[File:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), entrance.jpg|280px]]
Mound and stone chamber entrance |Other_name= |Group={{ill|Oiwayama Kofun Group|ja|大岩山古墳群}} |location={{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}{{ill|Yasu|ja|野洲市}} Koshinohara (Azamaruyama)
(Sakuraba Historic Park) |Lat_deg=35|Lat_min=4|Lat_sec=26.82 |Lon_deg=136|Lon_min=2|Lon_sec=28.35 |ISO=JP-25 |Shape={{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}} |Scale=Diameter 28m
Height 8m |Burial_facility=One-sided {{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室}}
(Two house-shaped sarcophagi inside) |Artifacts=Numerous grave goods |Accompanying_mounds= |Construction_period=Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} (or early 6th century) |Buried_person= |Imperial_tomb= |Cultural_property=Included in the national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} "Oiwayama Kofun Group" |Tangible_cultural_property= |Special_notes= |Map=Japan Shiga |Icon=円墳 |Label=Maruyama Kofun |Label_position=bottom }} '''Maruyama Kofun''' (円山古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Yasu|ja|野洲市}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}, in the Koshinohara area. It has a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}} shape and is one of the burial mounds that comprise the {{ill|Ōiwayama Kofun Cluster|ja|大岩山古墳群}}, designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}}. == Overview == This kofun was constructed at the northern tip of a hill extending from Oiwayama to the right bank of the Yasu River in southern Shiga Prefecture. While the mound has been subject to looting, recent excavations were conducted as part of historic site maintenance efforts. The mound is circular, measuring 28 meters in diameter and approximately 8 meters in height {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}{{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. Initially thought to be a {{ill|Scallop-shaped burial mound|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}, it is now recognized as a round kofun {{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture Cultural Property Learning Sheet|2004}}. The burial facility consists of a one-sided {{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室}} opening westward {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Inside the chamber are two house-shaped sarcophagi: one made of Aso welded tuff (Aso pink stone/Makado stone) and another made of {{ill|Futagoyama|ja|二上山}} tuff. These sarcophagi, along with a variety of grave goods such as silver ear ornaments, iron weapons, and over 10,000 glass beads, were uncovered during excavations {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}{{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture Cultural Property Learning Sheet|2004}}. The construction period is estimated to be the early 6th century, placing it in the late Kofun period {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. It is believed to be the tomb of a regional leader and represents the first use of Aso welded tuff sarcophagi in the Yasu River basin {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. The burial goods reflect the power and influence of the entombed individual. The kofun site was designated a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} in 1941. It has since been preserved and opened to the public as part of the Sakuraba Historic Park, though access to the stone chamber is restricted and allowed only during annual autumn openings. == Archaeological History == * **1941**: Designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} (December 13) {{National Cultural Heritage Database|401|1579|Oiwayama Kofun Group}}. * **1982**: Surveyed {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. * **1985**: Six additional mounds were incorporated into the previously designated historic sites "Maruyama Kofun" and "Kozan Kofun," leading to the renaming of the designation as "Oiwayama Kofun Group" . * **2001**: Excavations and maintenance reports by the Yasu Town Board of Education. * **2001**: Sakuraba Historic Park opened on November 3 {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the burial chamber}}]] [[File:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the burial chamber}}]] The burial facility is a one-sided {{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the west {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Its dimensions are as follows {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}{{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}: * Total length: 10.8 meters * Chamber: Length 4.3 meters, width 2.4 meters, height 3.1 meters * Passage: Length 6.5 meters, width 1.4 meters The passage descends approximately 1 meter from the entrance to the chamber floor, with the ceiling stones also stepping down {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The chamber floor is paved with pebbles {{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture Cultural Property Learning Sheet|2004}}. Inside the chamber are two sarcophagi: * **Hollowed-out house-shaped sarcophagus**: Located near the front of the chamber and presumed to be the primary burial. Made of Aso welded tuff (Aso pink stone/Makado stone), it measures 2.85 meters in length, 1.46 meters in width, and 1.83 meters in height. The lid stone has rope-loop projections and is painted with red pigment {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. * **Assembled house-shaped sarcophagus**: Positioned at the back of the chamber, made of Futagoyama tuff. Its lid and front stones are damaged, but the base stone measures 2.45 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width, while the sarcophagus body is about 0.8 meters in height. This is considered one of the oldest examples of Futagoyama tuff sarcophagi in Shiga Prefecture {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), genshitsu-2.jpg|Chamber (facing rear wall) Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), sendou-2.jpg|Passage (facing entrance) Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), sendou-1.jpg|Passage (facing chamber) == Artifacts == Notable grave goods recovered include {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}: * **Ornaments** * Silver ear ornaments with pendants * Silver spiral earrings * Step pendants * Silver hollow beads * ~10,000 glass beads {{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture Cultural Property Learning Sheet|2004}} * **Weapons** * Lamellar armor * Helmet with horizontal riveting * Sword with twisted ring pommel * Silver spiral fittings * Small knife with antler handle * Over 10 iron spearheads * Over 500 iron arrowheads * Quiver * **Horse gear** * Saddle fittings * Stirrups * Bits == Related Facilities == * Sakuraba Historic Park Information Center (Yasu City, Koshinohara) * Yasu City Museum of History and Folklore (Dotaku Museum, Yasu City, Tsuji Town) == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{PDFlink|[https://www.yasu-kankou.com/common/files/pamph_07.pdf Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet]}} (Yasu City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Section, Sakuraba Historic Park Information Center, linked from Yasu City Tourism Association) {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. * Explanation boards (former Yasu Town Board of Education, 2001). * Prefectural publications: * {{PDFlink|[http://www.shiga-ec.ed.jp/www/contents/1438304524592/html/common/other/55d173d3025.pdf "Oiwayama Kofun Group"]}} Shiga Prefectural Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Section, 2004. * Encyclopedias: * {{Cite book|title=Maruyama Kofun|series=Continued Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Shuppan|year=2002}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Ōiwayama Kofun Cluster|ja|大岩山古墳群}} [[Category:Kofun in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yasu]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:01-17]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[ja:円山古墳 (野洲市)]]

Height 8m |Burial_facility=One-sided {{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室}}
(Two house-shaped sarcophagi inside) |Artifacts=Numerous grave goods |Accompanying_mounds= |Construction_period=Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} (or early 6th century) |Buried_person= |Imperial_tomb= |Cultural_property=Included in the national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} "Oiwayama Kofun Group" |Tangible_cultural_property= |Special_notes= |Map=Japan Shiga |Icon=円墳 |Label=Maruyama Kofun |Label_position=bottom }} '''Maruyama Kofun''' (円山古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Yasu|ja|野洲市}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}, in the Koshinohara area. It has a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}} shape and is one of the burial mounds that comprise the {{ill|Ōiwayama Kofun Cluster|ja|大岩山古墳群}}, designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}}. == Overview == This kofun was constructed at the northern tip of a hill extending from Oiwayama to the right bank of the Yasu River in southern Shiga Prefecture. While the mound has been subject to looting, recent excavations were conducted as part of historic site maintenance efforts. The mound is circular, measuring 28 meters in diameter and approximately 8 meters in height {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}{{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. Initially thought to be a {{ill|Scallop-shaped burial mound|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}, it is now recognized as a round kofun {{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture Cultural Property Learning Sheet|2004}}. The burial facility consists of a one-sided {{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室}} opening westward {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Inside the chamber are two house-shaped sarcophagi: one made of Aso welded tuff (Aso pink stone/Makado stone) and another made of {{ill|Futagoyama|ja|二上山}} tuff. These sarcophagi, along with a variety of grave goods such as silver ear ornaments, iron weapons, and over 10,000 glass beads, were uncovered during excavations {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}{{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture Cultural Property Learning Sheet|2004}}. The construction period is estimated to be the early 6th century, placing it in the late Kofun period {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. It is believed to be the tomb of a regional leader and represents the first use of Aso welded tuff sarcophagi in the Yasu River basin {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. The burial goods reflect the power and influence of the entombed individual. The kofun site was designated a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} in 1941. It has since been preserved and opened to the public as part of the Sakuraba Historic Park, though access to the stone chamber is restricted and allowed only during annual autumn openings. == Archaeological History == * **1941**: Designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} (December 13) {{National Cultural Heritage Database|401|1579|Oiwayama Kofun Group}}. * **1982**: Surveyed {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. * **1985**: Six additional mounds were incorporated into the previously designated historic sites "Maruyama Kofun" and "Kozan Kofun," leading to the renaming of the designation as "Oiwayama Kofun Group" . * **2001**: Excavations and maintenance reports by the Yasu Town Board of Education. * **2001**: Sakuraba Historic Park opened on November 3 {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the burial chamber}}]] [[File:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the burial chamber}}]] The burial facility is a one-sided {{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the west {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Its dimensions are as follows {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}{{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}: * Total length: 10.8 meters * Chamber: Length 4.3 meters, width 2.4 meters, height 3.1 meters * Passage: Length 6.5 meters, width 1.4 meters The passage descends approximately 1 meter from the entrance to the chamber floor, with the ceiling stones also stepping down {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The chamber floor is paved with pebbles {{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture Cultural Property Learning Sheet|2004}}. Inside the chamber are two sarcophagi: * **Hollowed-out house-shaped sarcophagus**: Located near the front of the chamber and presumed to be the primary burial. Made of Aso welded tuff (Aso pink stone/Makado stone), it measures 2.85 meters in length, 1.46 meters in width, and 1.83 meters in height. The lid stone has rope-loop projections and is painted with red pigment {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. * **Assembled house-shaped sarcophagus**: Positioned at the back of the chamber, made of Futagoyama tuff. Its lid and front stones are damaged, but the base stone measures 2.45 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width, while the sarcophagus body is about 0.8 meters in height. This is considered one of the oldest examples of Futagoyama tuff sarcophagi in Shiga Prefecture {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), genshitsu-2.jpg|Chamber (facing rear wall) Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), sendou-2.jpg|Passage (facing entrance) Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), sendou-1.jpg|Passage (facing chamber) == Artifacts == Notable grave goods recovered include {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}: * **Ornaments** * Silver ear ornaments with pendants * Silver spiral earrings * Step pendants * Silver hollow beads * ~10,000 glass beads {{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture Cultural Property Learning Sheet|2004}} * **Weapons** * Lamellar armor * Helmet with horizontal riveting * Sword with twisted ring pommel * Silver spiral fittings * Small knife with antler handle * Over 10 iron spearheads * Over 500 iron arrowheads * Quiver * **Horse gear** * Saddle fittings * Stirrups * Bits == Related Facilities == * Sakuraba Historic Park Information Center (Yasu City, Koshinohara) * Yasu City Museum of History and Folklore (Dotaku Museum, Yasu City, Tsuji Town) == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{PDFlink|[https://www.yasu-kankou.com/common/files/pamph_07.pdf Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet]}} (Yasu City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Section, Sakuraba Historic Park Information Center, linked from Yasu City Tourism Association) {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. * Explanation boards (former Yasu Town Board of Education, 2001). * Prefectural publications: * {{PDFlink|[http://www.shiga-ec.ed.jp/www/contents/1438304524592/html/common/other/55d173d3025.pdf "Oiwayama Kofun Group"]}} Shiga Prefectural Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Section, 2004. * Encyclopedias: * {{Cite book|title=Maruyama Kofun|series=Continued Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Shuppan|year=2002}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Ōiwayama Kofun Cluster|ja|大岩山古墳群}} [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Kofun in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::History of Yasu]] attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Maruyama Kofun (Yasu)]][[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::pages needing attribution]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Maruyama Kofun (Yasu)]][[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::pages needing attribution]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::bump 1-25]] [[ja:円山古墳 (野洲市)]]

{{Infobox ancient site |name=Maruyama Kofun |image=[[File:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), entrance.jpg|280px]]
Mound and stone chamber entrance |Other_name= |Group={{ill|Oiwayama Kofun Group|ja|大岩山古墳群}} |location={{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}{{ill|Yasu|ja|野洲市}} Koshinohara (Azamaruyama)
(Sakuraba Historic Park) |Lat_deg=35|Lat_min=4|Lat_sec=26.82 |Lon_deg=136|Lon_min=2|Lon_sec=28.35 |ISO=JP-25 |Shape={{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}} |Scale=Diameter 28m
Height 8m |Burial_facility=One-sided {{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室}}
(Two house-shaped sarcophagi inside) |Artifacts=Numerous grave goods |Accompanying_mounds= |Construction_period=Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} (or early 6th century) |Buried_person= |Imperial_tomb= |Cultural_property=Included in the national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} "Oiwayama Kofun Group" |Tangible_cultural_property= |Special_notes= |Map=Japan Shiga |Icon=円墳 |Label=Maruyama Kofun |Label_position=bottom }} '''Maruyama Kofun''' (円山古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Yasu|ja|野洲市}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}, in the Koshinohara area. It has a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}} shape and is one of the burial mounds that comprise the {{ill|Ōiwayama Kofun Cluster|ja|大岩山古墳群}}, designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}}. == Overview == This kofun was constructed at the northern tip of a hill extending from Oiwayama to the right bank of the Yasu River in southern Shiga Prefecture. While the mound has been subject to looting, recent excavations were conducted as part of historic site maintenance efforts. The mound is circular, measuring 28 meters in diameter and approximately 8 meters in height {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}{{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. Initially thought to be a {{ill|Scallop-shaped burial mound|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}, it is now recognized as a round kofun {{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture Cultural Property Learning Sheet|2004}}. The burial facility consists of a one-sided {{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室}} opening westward {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Inside the chamber are two house-shaped sarcophagi: one made of Aso welded tuff (Aso pink stone/Makado stone) and another made of {{ill|Futagoyama|ja|二上山}} tuff. These sarcophagi, along with a variety of grave goods such as silver ear ornaments, iron weapons, and over 10,000 glass beads, were uncovered during excavations {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}{{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture Cultural Property Learning Sheet|2004}}. The construction period is estimated to be the early 6th century, placing it in the late Kofun period {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. It is believed to be the tomb of a regional leader and represents the first use of Aso welded tuff sarcophagi in the Yasu River basin {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. The burial goods reflect the power and influence of the entombed individual. The kofun site was designated a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} in 1941. It has since been preserved and opened to the public as part of the Sakuraba Historic Park, though access to the stone chamber is restricted and allowed only during annual autumn openings. == Archaeological History == * **1941**: Designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} (December 13) {{National Cultural Heritage Database|401|1579|Oiwayama Kofun Group}}. * **1982**: Surveyed {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. * **1985**: Six additional mounds were incorporated into the previously designated historic sites "Maruyama Kofun" and "Kozan Kofun," leading to the renaming of the designation as "Oiwayama Kofun Group" . * **2001**: Excavations and maintenance reports by the Yasu Town Board of Education. * **2001**: Sakuraba Historic Park opened on November 3 {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the burial chamber}}]] [[File:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the burial chamber}}]] The burial facility is a one-sided {{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the west {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Its dimensions are as follows {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}{{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}: * Total length: 10.8 meters * Chamber: Length 4.3 meters, width 2.4 meters, height 3.1 meters * Passage: Length 6.5 meters, width 1.4 meters The passage descends approximately 1 meter from the entrance to the chamber floor, with the ceiling stones also stepping down {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The chamber floor is paved with pebbles {{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture Cultural Property Learning Sheet|2004}}. Inside the chamber are two sarcophagi: * **Hollowed-out house-shaped sarcophagus**: Located near the front of the chamber and presumed to be the primary burial. Made of Aso welded tuff (Aso pink stone/Makado stone), it measures 2.85 meters in length, 1.46 meters in width, and 1.83 meters in height. The lid stone has rope-loop projections and is painted with red pigment {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. * **Assembled house-shaped sarcophagus**: Positioned at the back of the chamber, made of Futagoyama tuff. Its lid and front stones are damaged, but the base stone measures 2.45 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width, while the sarcophagus body is about 0.8 meters in height. This is considered one of the oldest examples of Futagoyama tuff sarcophagi in Shiga Prefecture {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), genshitsu-2.jpg|Chamber (facing rear wall) Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), sendou-2.jpg|Passage (facing entrance) Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), sendou-1.jpg|Passage (facing chamber) == Artifacts == Notable grave goods recovered include {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}: * **Ornaments** * Silver ear ornaments with pendants * Silver spiral earrings * Step pendants * Silver hollow beads * ~10,000 glass beads {{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture Cultural Property Learning Sheet|2004}} * **Weapons** * Lamellar armor * Helmet with horizontal riveting * Sword with twisted ring pommel * Silver spiral fittings * Small knife with antler handle * Over 10 iron spearheads * Over 500 iron arrowheads * Quiver * **Horse gear** * Saddle fittings * Stirrups * Bits == Related Facilities == * Sakuraba Historic Park Information Center (Yasu City, Koshinohara) * Yasu City Museum of History and Folklore (Dotaku Museum, Yasu City, Tsuji Town) == References == Sakuraba Historic Park Information Center, linked from Yasu City Tourism Association) {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. * Explanation boards (former Yasu Town Board of Education, 2001). * [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan]] {{Infobox ancient site |name=Maruyama Kofun |image=[[File:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), entrance.jpg|280px]]
Mound and stone chamber entrance |Other_name= |Group={{ill|Oiwayama Kofun Group|ja|大岩山古墳群}} |location={{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}{{ill|Yasu|ja|野洲市}} Koshinohara (Azamaruyama)
(Sakuraba Historic Park) |Lat_deg=35|Lat_min=4|Lat_sec=26.82 |Lon_deg=136|Lon_min=2|Lon_sec=28.35 |ISO=JP-25 |Shape={{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}} |Scale=Diameter 28m
Height 8m |Burial_facility=One-sided {{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室}}
(Two house-shaped sarcophagi inside) |Artifacts=Numerous grave goods |Accompanying_mounds= |Construction_period=Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} (or early 6th century) |Buried_person= |Imperial_tomb= |Cultural_property=Included in the national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} "Oiwayama Kofun Group" |Tangible_cultural_property= |Special_notes= |Map=Japan Shiga |Icon=円墳 |Label=Maruyama Kofun |Label_position=bottom }} '''Maruyama Kofun''' (円山古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Yasu|ja|野洲市}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}, in the Koshinohara area. It has a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}} shape and is one of the burial mounds that comprise the {{ill|Ōiwayama Kofun Cluster|ja|大岩山古墳群}}, designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}}. == Overview == This kofun was constructed at the northern tip of a hill extending from Oiwayama to the right bank of the Yasu River in southern Shiga Prefecture. While the mound has been subject to looting, recent excavations were conducted as part of historic site maintenance efforts. The mound is circular, measuring 28 meters in diameter and approximately 8 meters in height {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}{{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. Initially thought to be a {{ill|Scallop-shaped burial mound|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}, it is now recognized as a round kofun {{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture Cultural Property Learning Sheet|2004}}. The burial facility consists of a one-sided {{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室}} opening westward {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Inside the chamber are two house-shaped sarcophagi: one made of Aso welded tuff (Aso pink stone/Makado stone) and another made of {{ill|Futagoyama|ja|二上山}} tuff. These sarcophagi, along with a variety of grave goods such as silver ear ornaments, iron weapons, and over 10,000 glass beads, were uncovered during excavations {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}{{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture Cultural Property Learning Sheet|2004}}. The construction period is estimated to be the early 6th century, placing it in the late Kofun period {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. It is believed to be the tomb of a regional leader and represents the first use of Aso welded tuff sarcophagi in the Yasu River basin {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. The burial goods reflect the power and influence of the entombed individual. The kofun site was designated a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} in 1941. It has since been preserved and opened to the public as part of the Sakuraba Historic Park, though access to the stone chamber is restricted and allowed only during annual autumn openings. == Archaeological History == * **1941**: Designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} (December 13) {{National Cultural Heritage Database|401|1579|Oiwayama Kofun Group}}. * **1982**: Surveyed {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. * **1985**: Six additional mounds were incorporated into the previously designated historic sites "Maruyama Kofun" and "Kozan Kofun," leading to the renaming of the designation as "Oiwayama Kofun Group" . * **2001**: Excavations and maintenance reports by the Yasu Town Board of Education. * **2001**: Sakuraba Historic Park opened on November 3 {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the burial chamber}}]] [[File:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the burial chamber}}]] The burial facility is a one-sided {{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the west {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Its dimensions are as follows {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}{{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}: * Total length: 10.8 meters * Chamber: Length 4.3 meters, width 2.4 meters, height 3.1 meters * Passage: Length 6.5 meters, width 1.4 meters The passage descends approximately 1 meter from the entrance to the chamber floor, with the ceiling stones also stepping down {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The chamber floor is paved with pebbles {{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture Cultural Property Learning Sheet|2004}}. Inside the chamber are two sarcophagi: * **Hollowed-out house-shaped sarcophagus**: Located near the front of the chamber and presumed to be the primary burial. Made of Aso welded tuff (Aso pink stone/Makado stone), it measures 2.85 meters in length, 1.46 meters in width, and 1.83 meters in height. The lid stone has rope-loop projections and is painted with red pigment {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. * **Assembled house-shaped sarcophagus**: Positioned at the back of the chamber, made of Futagoyama tuff. Its lid and front stones are damaged, but the base stone measures 2.45 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width, while the sarcophagus body is about 0.8 meters in height. This is considered one of the oldest examples of Futagoyama tuff sarcophagi in Shiga Prefecture {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), genshitsu-2.jpg|Chamber (facing rear wall) Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), sendou-2.jpg|Passage (facing entrance) Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), sendou-1.jpg|Passage (facing chamber) == Artifacts == Notable grave goods recovered include {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}: * **Ornaments** * Silver ear ornaments with pendants * Silver spiral earrings * Step pendants * Silver hollow beads * ~10,000 glass beads {{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture Cultural Property Learning Sheet|2004}} * **Weapons** * Lamellar armor * Helmet with horizontal riveting * Sword with twisted ring pommel * Silver spiral fittings * Small knife with antler handle * Over 10 iron spearheads * Over 500 iron arrowheads * Quiver * **Horse gear** * Saddle fittings * Stirrups * Bits == Related Facilities == * Sakuraba Historic Park Information Center (Yasu City, Koshinohara) * Yasu City Museum of History and Folklore (Dotaku Museum, Yasu City, Tsuji Town) == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{PDFlink|[https://www.yasu-kankou.com/common/files/pamph_07.pdf Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet]}} (Yasu City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Section, Sakuraba Historic Park Information Center, linked from Yasu City Tourism Association) {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. * Explanation boards (former Yasu Town Board of Education, 2001). * Prefectural publications: * {{PDFlink|[http://www.shiga-ec.ed.jp/www/contents/1438304524592/html/common/other/55d173d3025.pdf "Oiwayama Kofun Group"]}} Shiga Prefectural Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Section, 2004. * Encyclopedias: * {{Cite book|title=Maruyama Kofun|series=Continued Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Shuppan|year=2002}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Ōiwayama Kofun Cluster|ja|大岩山古墳群}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Yasu]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan]] attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu)]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:01-17]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu)]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:kk]] {{Infobox ancient site |name=Maruyama Kofun |image=[[File:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), entrance.jpg|280px]]
Mound and stone chamber entrance |Other_name= |Group={{ill|Oiwayama Kofun Group|ja|大岩山古墳群}} |location={{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}{{ill|Yasu|ja|野洲市}} Koshinohara (Azamaruyama)
(Sakuraba Historic Park) |Lat_deg=35|Lat_min=4|Lat_sec=26.82 |Lon_deg=136|Lon_min=2|Lon_sec=28.35 |ISO=JP-25 |Shape={{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}} |Scale=Diameter 28m
Height 8m |Burial_facility=One-sided {{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室}}
(Two house-shaped sarcophagi inside) |Artifacts=Numerous grave goods |Accompanying_mounds= |Construction_period=Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} (or early 6th century) |Buried_person= |Imperial_tomb= |Cultural_property=Included in the national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} "Oiwayama Kofun Group" |Tangible_cultural_property= |Special_notes= |Map=Japan Shiga |Icon=円墳 |Label=Maruyama Kofun |Label_position=bottom }} '''Maruyama Kofun''' (円山古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Yasu|ja|野洲市}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}, in the Koshinohara area. It has a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}} shape and is one of the burial mounds that comprise the {{ill|Ōiwayama Kofun Cluster|ja|大岩山古墳群}}, designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}}. == Overview == This kofun was constructed at the northern tip of a hill extending from Oiwayama to the right bank of the Yasu River in southern Shiga Prefecture. While the mound has been subject to looting, recent excavations were conducted as part of historic site maintenance efforts. The mound is circular, measuring 28 meters in diameter and approximately 8 meters in height {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}{{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. Initially thought to be a {{ill|Scallop-shaped burial mound|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}, it is now recognized as a round kofun {{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture Cultural Property Learning Sheet|2004}}. The burial facility consists of a one-sided {{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室}} opening westward {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Inside the chamber are two house-shaped sarcophagi: one made of Aso welded tuff (Aso pink stone/Makado stone) and another made of {{ill|Futagoyama|ja|二上山}} tuff. These sarcophagi, along with a variety of grave goods such as silver ear ornaments, iron weapons, and over 10,000 glass beads, were uncovered during excavations {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}{{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture Cultural Property Learning Sheet|2004}}. The construction period is estimated to be the early 6th century, placing it in the late Kofun period {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. It is believed to be the tomb of a regional leader and represents the first use of Aso welded tuff sarcophagi in the Yasu River basin {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. The burial goods reflect the power and influence of the entombed individual. The kofun site was designated a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} in 1941. It has since been preserved and opened to the public as part of the Sakuraba Historic Park, though access to the stone chamber is restricted and allowed only during annual autumn openings. == Archaeological History == * **1941**: Designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} (December 13) {{National Cultural Heritage Database|401|1579|Oiwayama Kofun Group}}. * **1982**: Surveyed {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. * **1985**: Six additional mounds were incorporated into the previously designated historic sites "Maruyama Kofun" and "Kozan Kofun," leading to the renaming of the designation as "Oiwayama Kofun Group" . * **2001**: Excavations and maintenance reports by the Yasu Town Board of Education. * **2001**: Sakuraba Historic Park opened on November 3 {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the burial chamber}}]] [[File:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the burial chamber}}]] The burial facility is a one-sided {{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the west {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Its dimensions are as follows {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}{{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}: * Total length: 10.8 meters * Chamber: Length 4.3 meters, width 2.4 meters, height 3.1 meters * Passage: Length 6.5 meters, width 1.4 meters The passage descends approximately 1 meter from the entrance to the chamber floor, with the ceiling stones also stepping down {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The chamber floor is paved with pebbles {{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture Cultural Property Learning Sheet|2004}}. Inside the chamber are two sarcophagi: * **Hollowed-out house-shaped sarcophagus**: Located near the front of the chamber and presumed to be the primary burial. Made of Aso welded tuff (Aso pink stone/Makado stone), it measures 2.85 meters in length, 1.46 meters in width, and 1.83 meters in height. The lid stone has rope-loop projections and is painted with red pigment {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. * **Assembled house-shaped sarcophagus**: Positioned at the back of the chamber, made of Futagoyama tuff. Its lid and front stones are damaged, but the base stone measures 2.45 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width, while the sarcophagus body is about 0.8 meters in height. This is considered one of the oldest examples of Futagoyama tuff sarcophagi in Shiga Prefecture {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), genshitsu-2.jpg|Chamber (facing rear wall) Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), sendou-2.jpg|Passage (facing entrance) Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), sendou-1.jpg|Passage (facing chamber) == Artifacts == Notable grave goods recovered include {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}: * **Ornaments** * Silver ear ornaments with pendants * Silver spiral earrings * Step pendants * Silver hollow beads * ~10,000 glass beads {{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture Cultural Property Learning Sheet|2004}} * **Weapons** * Lamellar armor * Helmet with horizontal riveting * Sword with twisted ring pommel * Silver spiral fittings * Small knife with antler handle * Over 10 iron spearheads * Over 500 iron arrowheads * Quiver * **Horse gear** * Saddle fittings * Stirrups * Bits == Artifacts == Notable grave goods recovered include {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}: * **Ornaments** * Silver ear ornaments with pendants * Silver spiral earrings * Step pendants * Silver hollow beads * ~10,000 glass beads {{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture Cultural Property Learning Sheet|2004}} * **Weapons** * Lamellar armor * Helmet with horizontal riveting * Sword with twisted ring pommel * Silver spiral fittings * Small knife with antler handle * Over 10 iron spearheads * Over 500 iron arrowheads * Quiver * **Horse gear** * Saddle fittings * Stirrups * Bits == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Maruyama Kofun |image=[[File:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), entrance.jpg|280px]]
Mound and stone chamber entrance |Other_name= |Group={{ill|Oiwayama Kofun Group|ja|大岩山古墳群}} |location={{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}{{ill|Yasu|ja|野洲市}} Koshinohara (Azamaruyama)
(Sakuraba Historic Park) |Lat_deg=35|Lat_min=4|Lat_sec=26.82 |Lon_deg=136|Lon_min=2|Lon_sec=28.35 |ISO=JP-25 |Shape={{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}} |Scale=Diameter 28m
Height 8m |Burial_facility=One-sided {{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室}}
(Two house-shaped sarcophagi inside) |Artifacts=Numerous grave goods |Accompanying_mounds= |Construction_period=Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} (or early 6th century) |Buried_person= |Imperial_tomb= |Cultural_property=Included in the national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} "Oiwayama Kofun Group" |Tangible_cultural_property= |Special_notes= |Map=Japan Shiga |Icon=円墳 |Label=Maruyama Kofun |Label_position=bottom }} '''Maruyama Kofun''' (円山古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Yasu|ja|野洲市}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}, in the Koshinohara area. It has a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}} shape and is one of the burial mounds that comprise the {{ill|Ōiwayama Kofun Cluster|ja|大岩山古墳群}}, designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}}. == Overview == This kofun was constructed at the northern tip of a hill extending from Oiwayama to the right bank of the Yasu River in southern Shiga Prefecture. While the mound has been subject to looting, recent excavations were conducted as part of historic site maintenance efforts. The mound is circular, measuring 28 meters in diameter and approximately 8 meters in height {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}{{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. Initially thought to be a {{ill|Scallop-shaped burial mound|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}, it is now recognized as a round kofun {{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture Cultural Property Learning Sheet|2004}}. The burial facility consists of a one-sided {{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室}} opening westward {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Inside the chamber are two house-shaped sarcophagi: one made of Aso welded tuff (Aso pink stone/Makado stone) and another made of {{ill|Futagoyama|ja|二上山}} tuff. These sarcophagi, along with a variety of grave goods such as silver ear ornaments, iron weapons, and over 10,000 glass beads, were uncovered during excavations {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}{{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture Cultural Property Learning Sheet|2004}}. The construction period is estimated to be the early 6th century, placing it in the late Kofun period {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. It is believed to be the tomb of a regional leader and represents the first use of Aso welded tuff sarcophagi in the Yasu River basin {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. The burial goods reflect the power and influence of the entombed individual. The kofun site was designated a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} in 1941. It has since been preserved and opened to the public as part of the Sakuraba Historic Park, though access to the stone chamber is restricted and allowed only during annual autumn openings. == Archaeological History == * **1941**: Designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} (December 13) {{National Cultural Heritage Database|401|1579|Oiwayama Kofun Group}}. * **1982**: Surveyed {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. * **1985**: Six additional mounds were incorporated into the previously designated historic sites "Maruyama Kofun" and "Kozan Kofun," leading to the renaming of the designation as "Oiwayama Kofun Group" . * **2001**: Excavations and maintenance reports by the Yasu Town Board of Education. * **2001**: Sakuraba Historic Park opened on November 3 {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the burial chamber}}]] [[File:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the burial chamber}}]] The burial facility is a one-sided {{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the west {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Its dimensions are as follows {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}{{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}: * Total length: 10.8 meters * Chamber: Length 4.3 meters, width 2.4 meters, height 3.1 meters * Passage: Length 6.5 meters, width 1.4 meters The passage descends approximately 1 meter from the entrance to the chamber floor, with the ceiling stones also stepping down {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The chamber floor is paved with pebbles {{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture Cultural Property Learning Sheet|2004}}. Inside the chamber are two sarcophagi: * **Hollowed-out house-shaped sarcophagus**: Located near the front of the chamber and presumed to be the primary burial. Made of Aso welded tuff (Aso pink stone/Makado stone), it measures 2.85 meters in length, 1.46 meters in width, and 1.83 meters in height. The lid stone has rope-loop projections and is painted with red pigment {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. * **Assembled house-shaped sarcophagus**: Positioned at the back of the chamber, made of Futagoyama tuff. Its lid and front stones are damaged, but the base stone measures 2.45 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width, while the sarcophagus body is about 0.8 meters in height. This is considered one of the oldest examples of Futagoyama tuff sarcophagi in Shiga Prefecture {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), genshitsu-2.jpg|Chamber (facing rear wall) Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), sendou-2.jpg|Passage (facing entrance) Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), sendou-1.jpg|Passage (facing chamber) == Artifacts == Notable grave goods recovered include {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}: * **Ornaments** * Silver ear ornaments with pendants * Silver spiral earrings * Step pendants * Silver hollow beads * ~10,000 glass beads {{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture Cultural Property Learning Sheet|2004}} * **Weapons** * Lamellar armor * Helmet with horizontal riveting * Sword with twisted ring pommel * Silver spiral fittings * Small knife with antler handle * Over 10 iron spearheads * Over 500 iron arrowheads * Quiver * **Horse gear** * Saddle fittings * Stirrups * Bits == Related Facilities == * Sakuraba Historic Park Information Center (Yasu City, Koshinohara) * Yasu City Museum of History and Folklore (Dotaku Museum, Yasu City, Tsuji Town) == References == Sakuraba Historic Park Information Center, linked from Yasu City Tourism Association) {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. * Explanation boards (former Yasu Town Board of Education, 2001). * Prefectural publications: * {{PDFlink|[http://www.shiga-ec.ed.jp/www/contents/1438304524592/html/common/other/55d173d3025.pdf "Oiwayama Kofun Group"]}} Shiga Prefectural Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Section, 2004. * Encyclopedias: * {{Cite book|title=Maruyama Kofun|series=Continued Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Shuppan|year=2002}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Ōiwayama The burial goods reflect the power and influence of the entombed individual. The kofun site was designated a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} in 1941. It has since been preserved and opened to the public as part of the Sakuraba Historic Park, though access to the stone chamber is restricted and allowed only during annual autumn openings. == Archaeological History == * **1941**: Designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} (December 13) {{National Cultural Heritage Database|401|1579|Oiwayama Kofun Group}}. * **1982**: Surveyed {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. * **1985**: Six additional mounds were incorporated into the previously designated historic sites "Maruyama Kofun" and "Kozan Kofun," leading to the renaming of the designation as "Oiwayama Kofun Group" . * **2001**: Excavations and maintenance reports by the Yasu Town Board of Education. * **2001**: Sakuraba Historic Park opened on November 3 {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the burial chamber}}]] [[File:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the burial chamber}}]] The burial facility is a one-sided {{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the west {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Its dimensions are as follows {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}{{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}: * Total length: 10.8 meters * Chamber: Length 4.3 meters, width 2.4 meters, height 3.1 meters * Passage: Length 6.5 meters, width 1.4 meters The passage descends approximately 1 meter from the entrance to the chamber floor, with the ceiling stones also stepping down {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The chamber floor is paved with pebbles {{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture Cultural Property Learning Sheet|2004}}. Inside the chamber are two sarcophagi: * **Hollowed-out house-shaped sarcophagus**: Located near the front of the chamber and presumed to be the primary burial. Made of Aso welded tuff (Aso pink stone/Makado stone), it measures 2.85 meters in length, 1.46 meters in width, and 1.83 meters in height. The lid stone has rope-loop projections and is painted with red pigment {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. * **Assembled house-shaped sarcophagus**: Positioned at the back of the chamber, made of Futagoyama tuff. Its lid and front stones are damaged, but the base stone measures 2.45 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width, while the sarcophagus body is about 0.8 meters in height. This is considered one of the oldest examples of Futagoyama tuff sarcophagi in Shiga Prefecture {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), genshitsu-2.jpg|Chamber (facing rear wall) Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), sendou-2.jpg|Passage (facing entrance) Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), sendou-1.jpg|Passage (facing chamber) == Artifacts == Notable grave goods recovered include {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}: * **Ornaments** * Silver ear ornaments with pendants * Silver spiral earrings * Step pendants * Silver hollow beads * ~10,000 glass beads {{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture Cultural Property Learning Sheet|2004}} * **Weapons** * Lamellar armor * Helmet with horizontal riveting * Sword with twisted ring pommel * Silver spiral fittings * Small knife with antler handle * Over 10 iron spearheads * Over 500 iron arrowheads * Quiver * **Horse gear** * Saddle fittings * Stirrups * Bits == Related Facilities == * Sakuraba Historic Park Information Center (Yasu City, Koshinohara) * Yasu City Museum of History and Folklore (Dotaku Museum, Yasu City, Tsuji Town) == References == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Maruyama Kofun |image=[[File:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), entrance.jpg|280px]]
Mound and stone chamber entrance |Other_name= |Group={{ill|Oiwayama Kofun Group|ja|大岩山古墳群}} |location={{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}{{ill|Yasu|ja|野洲市}} Koshinohara (Azamaruyama)
(Sakuraba Historic Park) |Lat_deg=35|Lat_min=4|Lat_sec=26.82 |Lon_deg=136|Lon_min=2|Lon_sec=28.35 |ISO=JP-25 |Shape={{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}} |Scale=Diameter 28m
Height 8m |Burial_facility=One-sided {{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室}}
(Two house-shaped sarcophagi inside) |Artifacts=Numerous grave goods |Accompanying_mounds= |Construction_period=Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} (or early 6th century) |Buried_person= |Imperial_tomb= |Cultural_property=Included in the national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} "Oiwayama Kofun Group" |Tangible_cultural_property= |Special_notes= |Map=Japan Shiga |Icon=円墳 |Label=Maruyama Kofun |Label_position=bottom }} '''Maruyama Kofun''' (円山古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Yasu|ja|野洲市}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}, in the Koshinohara area. It has a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}} shape and is one of the burial mounds that comprise the {{ill|Ōiwayama Kofun Cluster|ja|大岩山古墳群}}, designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}}. == Overview == This kofun was constructed at the northern tip of a hill extending from Oiwayama to the right bank of the Yasu River in southern Shiga Prefecture. While the mound has been subject to looting, recent excavations were conducted as part of historic site maintenance efforts. The mound is circular, measuring 28 meters in diameter and approximately 8 meters in height {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}{{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. Initially thought to be a {{ill|Scallop-shaped burial mound|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}, it is now recognized as a round kofun {{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture Cultural Property Learning Sheet|2004}}. The burial facility consists of a one-sided {{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室}} opening westward {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Inside the chamber are two house-shaped sarcophagi: one made of Aso welded tuff (Aso pink stone/Makado stone) and another made of {{ill|Futagoyama|ja|二上山}} tuff. These sarcophagi, along with a variety of grave goods such as silver ear ornaments, iron weapons, and over 10,000 glass beads, were uncovered during excavations {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}{{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture Cultural Property Learning Sheet|2004}}. The construction period is estimated to be the early 6th century, placing it in the late Kofun period {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. It is believed to be the tomb of a regional leader and represents the first use of Aso welded tuff sarcophagi in the Yasu River basin {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. The burial goods reflect the power and influence of the entombed individual. The kofun site was designated a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} in 1941. It has since been preserved and opened to the public as part of the Sakuraba Historic Park, though access to the stone chamber is restricted and allowed only during annual autumn openings. == Archaeological History == * **1941**: Designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} (December 13) {{National Cultural Heritage Database|401|1579|Oiwayama Kofun Group}}. * **1982**: Surveyed {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. * **1985**: Six additional mounds were incorporated into the previously designated historic sites "Maruyama Kofun" and "Kozan Kofun," leading to the renaming of the designation as "Oiwayama Kofun Group" . * **2001**: Excavations and maintenance reports by the Yasu Town Board of Education. * **2001**: Sakuraba Historic Park opened on November 3 {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the burial chamber}}]] [[File:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the burial chamber}}]] The burial facility is a one-sided {{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the west {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Its dimensions are as follows {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}{{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}: * Total length: 10.8 meters * Chamber: Length 4.3 meters, width 2.4 meters, height 3.1 meters * Passage: Length 6.5 meters, width 1.4 meters The passage descends approximately 1 meter from the entrance to the chamber floor, with the ceiling stones also stepping down {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The chamber floor is paved with pebbles {{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture Cultural Property Learning Sheet|2004}}. Inside the chamber are two sarcophagi: * **Hollowed-out house-shaped sarcophagus**: Located near the front of the chamber and presumed to be the primary burial. Made of Aso welded tuff (Aso pink stone/Makado stone), it measures 2.85 meters in length, 1.46 meters in width, and 1.83 meters in height. The lid stone has rope-loop projections and is painted with red pigment {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. * **Assembled house-shaped sarcophagus**: Positioned at the back of the chamber, made of Futagoyama tuff. Its lid and front stones are damaged, but the base stone measures 2.45 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width, while the sarcophagus body is about 0.8 meters in height. This is considered one of the oldest examples of Futagoyama tuff sarcophagi in Shiga Prefecture {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. (Two house-shaped sarcophagi inside) |Artifacts=Numerous grave goods |Accompanying_mounds= |Construction_period=Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} (or early 6th century) |Buried_person= |Imperial_tomb= |Cultural_property=Included in the national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} "Oiwayama Kofun Group" |Tangible_cultural_property= |Special_notes= |Map=Japan Shiga |Icon=円墳 |Label=Maruyama Kofun |Label_position=bottom }} '''Maruyama Kofun''' (円山古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Yasu|ja|野洲市}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}, in the Koshinohara area. It has a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}} shape and is one of the burial mounds that comprise the {{ill|Ōiwayama Kofun Cluster|ja|大岩山古墳群}}, designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}}. == Overview == This kofun was constructed at the northern tip of a hill extending from Oiwayama to the right bank of the Yasu River in southern Shiga Prefecture. While the mound has been subject to looting, recent excavations were conducted as part of historic site maintenance efforts. The mound is circular, measuring 28 meters in diameter and approximately 8 meters in height {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}{{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. Initially thought to be a {{ill|Scallop-shaped burial mound|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}, it is now recognized as a round kofun {{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture Cultural Property Learning Sheet|2004}}. The burial facility consists of a one-sided {{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室}} opening westward {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Inside the chamber are two house-shaped sarcophagi: one made of Aso welded tuff (Aso pink stone/Makado stone) and another made of {{ill|Futagoyama|ja|二上山}} tuff. These sarcophagi, along with a variety of grave goods such as silver ear ornaments, iron weapons, and over 10,000 glass beads, were uncovered during excavations {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}{{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture Cultural Property Learning Sheet|2004}}. The construction period is estimated to be the early 6th century, placing it in the late Kofun period {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. It is believed to be the tomb of a regional leader and represents the first use of Aso welded tuff sarcophagi in the Yasu River basin {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. The burial goods reflect the power and influence of the entombed individual. The kofun site was designated a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} in 1941. It has since been preserved and opened to the public as part of the Sakuraba Historic Park, though access to the stone chamber is restricted and allowed only during annual autumn openings. == Archaeological History == * **1941**: Designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} (December 13) {{National Cultural Heritage Database|401|1579|Oiwayama Kofun Group}}. * **1982**: Surveyed {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. * **1985**: Six additional mounds were incorporated into the previously designated historic sites "Maruyama Kofun" and "Kozan Kofun," leading to the renaming of the designation as "Oiwayama Kofun Group" . * **2001**: Excavations and maintenance reports by the Yasu Town Board of Education. * **2001**: Sakuraba Historic Park opened on November 3 {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the burial chamber}}]] [[File:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the burial chamber}}]] The burial facility is a one-sided {{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the west {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Its dimensions are as follows {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}{{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}: * Total length: 10.8 meters * Chamber: Length 4.3 meters, width 2.4 meters, height 3.1 meters * Passage: Length 6.5 meters, width 1.4 meters The passage descends approximately 1 meter from the entrance to the chamber floor, with the ceiling stones also stepping down {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The chamber floor is paved with pebbles {{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture Cultural Property Learning Sheet|2004}}. Inside the chamber are two sarcophagi: * **Hollowed-out house-shaped sarcophagus**: Located near the front of the chamber and presumed to be the primary burial. Made of Aso welded tuff (Aso pink stone/Makado stone), it measures 2.85 meters in length, 1.46 meters in width, and 1.83 meters in height. The lid stone has rope-loop projections and is painted with red pigment {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. * **Assembled house-shaped sarcophagus**: Positioned at the back of the chamber, made of Futagoyama tuff. Its lid and front stones are damaged, but the base stone measures 2.45 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width, while the sarcophagus body is about 0.8 meters in height. This is considered one of the oldest examples of Futagoyama tuff sarcophagi in Shiga Prefecture {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), genshitsu-2.jpg|Chamber (facing rear wall) Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), sendou-2.jpg|Passage (facing entrance) Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), sendou-1.jpg|Passage (facing chamber) == Artifacts == Notable grave goods recovered include {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}: * **Ornaments** * Silver ear ornaments with pendants * Silver spiral earrings * Step pendants * Silver hollow beads * ~10,000 glass beads {{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture Cultural Property Learning Sheet|2004}} * **Weapons** * Lamellar armor * Helmet with horizontal riveting * Sword with twisted ring pommel * Silver spiral fittings * Small knife with antler handle * Over 10 iron spearheads * Over 500 iron arrowheads * Quiver * **Horse gear** * Saddle fittings * Stirrups * Bits == Related Facilities == * Sakuraba Historic Park Information Center (Yasu City, Koshinohara) * Yasu City Museum of History and Folklore (Dotaku Museum, Yasu City, Tsuji Town) == References == {{Infobox ancient site |name=Maruyama Kofun |image=[[File:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), entrance.jpg|280px]]
Mound and stone chamber entrance |Other_name= |Group={{ill|Oiwayama Kofun Group|ja|大岩山古墳群}} |location={{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}{{ill|Yasu|ja|野洲市}} Koshinohara (Azamaruyama)
(Sakuraba Historic Park) |Lat_deg=35|Lat_min=4|Lat_sec=26.82 |Lon_deg=136|Lon_min=2|Lon_sec=28.35 |ISO=JP-25 |Shape={{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}} |Scale=Diameter 28m
Height 8m |Burial_facility=One-sided {{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室}}
(Two house-shaped sarcophagi inside) |Artifacts=Numerous grave goods |Accompanying_mounds= |Construction_period=Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} (or early 6th century) |Buried_person= |Imperial_tomb= |Cultural_property=Included in the national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} "Oiwayama Kofun Group" |Tangible_cultural_property= |Special_notes= |Map=Japan Shiga |Icon=円墳 |Label=Maruyama Kofun |Label_position=bottom }} '''Maruyama Kofun''' (円山古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Yasu|ja|野洲市}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}, in the Koshinohara area. It has a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}} shape and is one of the burial mounds that comprise the {{ill|Ōiwayama Kofun Cluster|ja|大岩山古墳群}}, designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}}. == Overview == This kofun was constructed at the northern tip of a hill extending from Oiwayama to the right bank of the Yasu River in southern Shiga Prefecture. While the mound has been subject to looting, recent excavations were conducted as part of historic site maintenance efforts. The mound is circular, measuring 28 meters in diameter and approximately 8 meters in height {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}{{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. Initially thought to be a {{ill|Scallop-shaped burial mound|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}, it is now recognized as a round kofun {{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture Cultural Property Learning Sheet|2004}}. The burial facility consists of a one-sided {{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室}} opening westward {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Inside the chamber are two house-shaped sarcophagi: one made of Aso welded tuff (Aso pink stone/Makado stone) and another made of {{ill|Futagoyama|ja|二上山}} tuff. These sarcophagi, along with a variety of grave goods such as silver ear ornaments, iron weapons, and over 10,000 glass beads, were uncovered during excavations {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}{{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture Cultural Property Learning Sheet|2004}}. The construction period is estimated to be the early 6th century, placing it in the late Kofun period {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. It is believed to be the tomb of a regional leader and represents the first use of Aso welded tuff sarcophagi in the Yasu River basin {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. The burial goods reflect the power and influence of the entombed individual. The kofun site was designated a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} in 1941. It has since been preserved and opened to the public as part of the Sakuraba Historic Park, though access to the stone chamber is restricted and allowed only during annual autumn openings. == Archaeological History == * **1941**: Designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} (December 13) {{National Cultural Heritage Database|401|1579|Oiwayama Kofun Group}}. * **1982**: Surveyed {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. * **1985**: Six additional mounds were incorporated into the previously designated historic sites "Maruyama Kofun" and "Kozan Kofun," leading to the renaming of the designation as "Oiwayama Kofun Group" . * **2001**: Excavations and maintenance reports by the Yasu Town Board of Education. * **2001**: Sakuraba Historic Park opened on November 3 {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the burial chamber}}]] [[File:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the burial chamber}}]] The burial facility is a one-sided {{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the west {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Its dimensions are as follows {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}{{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}: * Total length: 10.8 meters * Chamber: Length 4.3 meters, width 2.4 meters, height 3.1 meters * Passage: Length 6.5 meters, width 1.4 meters The passage descends approximately 1 meter from the entrance to the chamber floor, with the ceiling stones also stepping down {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The chamber floor is paved with pebbles {{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture Cultural Property Learning Sheet|2004}}. Inside the chamber are two sarcophagi: * **Hollowed-out house-shaped sarcophagus**: Located near the front of the chamber and presumed to be the primary burial. Made of Aso welded tuff (Aso pink stone/Makado stone), it measures 2.85 meters in length, 1.46 meters in width, and 1.83 meters in height. The lid stone has rope-loop projections and is painted with red pigment {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. * **Assembled house-shaped sarcophagus**: Positioned at the back of the chamber, made of Futagoyama tuff. Its lid and front stones are damaged, but the base stone measures 2.45 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width, while the sarcophagus body is about 0.8 meters in height. This is considered one of the oldest examples of Futagoyama tuff sarcophagi in Shiga Prefecture {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), genshitsu-2.jpg|Chamber (facing rear wall) Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), sendou-2.jpg|Passage (facing entrance) Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), sendou-1.jpg|Passage (facing chamber) == Artifacts == Notable grave goods recovered include {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}: * **Ornaments** * Silver ear ornaments with pendants * Silver spiral earrings * Step pendants * Silver hollow beads * ~10,000 glass beads {{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture Cultural Property Learning Sheet|2004}} * **Weapons** * Lamellar armor * Helmet with horizontal riveting * Sword with twisted ring pommel * Silver spiral fittings * Small knife with antler handle * Over 10 iron spearheads * Over 500 iron arrowheads * Quiver * **Horse gear** * Saddle fittings * Stirrups * Bits == Related Facilities == * Sakuraba Historic Park Information Center (Yasu City, Koshinohara) * Yasu City Museum of History and Folklore (Dotaku Museum, Yasu City, Tsuji Town) == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{PDFlink|[https://www.yasu-kankou.com/common/files/pamph_07.pdf Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet]}} (Yasu City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Section, Sakuraba Historic Park Information Center, linked from Yasu City Tourism Association) {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. * Explanation boards (former Yasu Town Board of Education, 2001). * Prefectural publications: * {{PDFlink|[http://www.shiga-ec.ed.jp/www/contents/1438304524592/html/common/other/55d173d3025.pdf "Oiwayama Kofun Group"]}} Shiga Prefectural Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Section, 2004. * Encyclopedias: * {{Cite book|title=Maruyama Kofun|series=Continued Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Shuppan|year=2002}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Ōiwayama Kofun Cluster|ja|大岩山古墳群}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Yasu]]
