{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} |name=Maruyama Kofun |image=[[File:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), entrance.jpg|280px]]
Mound and stone chamber entrance |Other_name= | part_of={{ill|Oiwayama Kofun Group|ja|大岩山古墳群|12=draft}} |location={{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|12=draft}}{{ill|Yasu|ja|野洲市|12=draft}} Koshinohara (Azamaruyama)
(Sakuraba Historic Park) |Lat_deg=35|Lat_min=4|Lat_sec=26.82 |Lon_deg=136|Lon_min=2|Lon_sec=28.35 |omitted=JP-25 |type={{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} |map_size=Diameter 28m
Height 8m |burial=One-sided {{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}
(Two house-shaped sarcophagi inside) |burial goods=Numerous grave goods |Accompanying_mounds= |built=Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft}} (or early 6th century) |Interred_individual= |Mausoleum= |Cultural_property=Included in the national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft}}} "Oiwayama Kofun Group" |Tangible_notes= |Special_notes= |map=Japan Shiga |type=円墳 |map_dot_label=Maruyama Kofun |map_dot_label=bottom }} '''Maruyama Kofun''' (円山古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Yasu|ja|野洲市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|12=draft}}, in the Koshinohara area. It has a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} type and is one of the Kofuns that comprise the {{ill|Ōiwayama Kofun Cluster|ja|大岩山古墳群|12=draft}}, Historic site as a national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft}}}. == Overview == This kofun was constructed at the northern tip of a hill extending from Oiwayama to the right bank of the Yasu River in southern Shiga Prefecture. While the mound has been subject to looting, recent excavations were conducted as part of historic site maintenance efforts. The mound is circular, measuring 28 meters in diameter and approximately 8 meters in height {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}{{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. Initially thought to be a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft}}, it is now recognized as a round kofun {{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture designation1 Learning Sheet|2004}}. The burial facility consists of a one-sided {{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} opening westward {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Inside the chamber are two house-shaped sarcophagi: one made of Aso welded tuff (Aso pink stone/Makado stone) and another made of {{ill|Futagoyama|ja|二上山|12=draft}} tuff. These sarcophagi, along with a variety of grave goods such as silver ear ornaments, iron weapons, and over 10,000 glass beads, were uncovered during excavations {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}{{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture designation1 Learning Sheet|2004}}. The construction period is estimated to be the early 6th century, placing it in the late Kofun period {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. It is believed to be the tomb of a regional leader and represents the first use of Aso welded tuff sarcophagi in the Yasu River basin {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. The burial goods reflect the power and influence of the entombed individual. The kofun site was Historic site a national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft}}} in 1941. It has since been preserved and opened to the public as part of the Sakuraba Historic Park, though access to the stone chamber is restricted and allowed only during annual autumn openings. == Archaeological History == * '''1941''': Designated as a national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft}}} (December 13) {{National Historic site Database|401|1579|Oiwayama Kofun Group}}. * '''1982''': Surveyed {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. * '''1985''': Six additional mounds were incorporated into the previously Historic site historic sites "Maruyama Kofun" and "Kozan Kofun," leading to the renaming of the Historic site as "Oiwayama Kofun Group" . * '''2001''': Excavations and maintenance reports by the Yasu Town Board of Education. * '''2001''': Sakuraba Historic Park opened on November 3 {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. == burial == [[File:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the burial chamber}}]] [[File:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the burial chamber}}]] The burial facility is a one-sided {{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} opening to the west {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Its area are as follows {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}{{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}: * Total length: 10.8 meters * Chamber: Length 4.3 meters, width 2.4 meters, height 3.1 meters * Passage: Length 6.5 meters, width 1.4 meters The passage descends approximately 1 meter from the entrance to the chamber floor, with the ceiling stones also stepping down {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The chamber floor is paved with pebbles {{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture designation1 Learning Sheet|2004}}. Inside the chamber are two sarcophagi: * '''Hollowed-out house-shaped sarcophagus''': Located near the front of the chamber and presumed to be the primary burial. Made of Aso welded tuff (Aso pink stone/Makado stone), it measures 2.85 meters in length, 1.46 meters in width, and 1.83 meters in height. The lid stone has rope-loop projections and is painted with red pigment {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. * '''Assembled house-shaped sarcophagus''': Positioned at the back of the chamber, made of Futagoyama tuff. Its lid and front stones are damaged, but the base stone measures 2.45 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width, while the sarcophagus body is about 0.8 meters in height. This is considered one of the oldest examples of Futagoyama tuff sarcophagi in Shiga Prefecture {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), genshitsu-2.jpg|Chamber (facing rear wall) Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), sendou-2.jpg|Passage (facing entrance) Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), sendou-1.jpg|Passage (facing chamber) == burial goods == Notable grave goods recovered include {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}: * '''Ornaments''' ** Silver ear ornaments with pendants ** Silver spiral earrings ** Step pendants ** Silver hollow beads ** ~10,000 glass beads {{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture designation1 Learning Sheet|2004}} * '''Weapons''' ** Lamellar armor ** Helmet with horizontal riveting ** Sword with twisted ring pommel ** Silver spiral fittings ** Small knife with antler handle ** Over 10 iron spearheads ** Over 500 iron arrowheads ** Quiver * '''Horse gear''' ** Saddle fittings ** Stirrups ** Mound and stone chamber entrance |Other_name= | part_of={{ill|Oiwayama Kofun Group|ja|大岩山古墳群|12=draft}} |location={{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|12=draft}}{{ill|Yasu|ja|野洲市|12=draft}} Koshinohara (Azamaruyama)
(Sakuraba Historic Park) |Lat_deg=35|Lat_min=4|Lat_sec=26.82 |Lon_deg=136|Lon_min=2|Lon_sec=28.35 |omitted=JP-25 |type={{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} |map_size=Diameter 28m
Height 8m |burial=One-sided {{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}
(Two house-shaped sarcophagi inside) |burial goods=Numerous grave goods |Accompanying_mounds= |built=Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft}} (or early 6th century) |Interred_individual= |Mausoleum= |Cultural_property=Included in the national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft}}} "Oiwayama Kofun Group" |Tangible_notes= |Special_notes= |map=Japan Shiga |type=円墳 |map_dot_label=Maruyama Kofun |map_dot_label=bottom }} '''Maruyama Kofun''' (円山古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Yasu|ja|野洲市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|12=draft}}, in the Koshinohara area. It has a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} type and is one of the Kofuns that comprise the {{ill|Ōiwayama Kofun Cluster|ja|大岩山古墳群|12=draft}}, Historic site as a national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft}}}. == Overview == This kofun was constructed at the northern tip of a hill extending from Oiwayama to the right bank of the Yasu River in southern Shiga Prefecture. While the mound has been subject to looting, recent excavations were conducted as part of historic site maintenance efforts. The mound is circular, measuring 28 meters in diameter and approximately 8 meters in height {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}{{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. Initially thought to be a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft}}, it is now recognized as a round kofun {{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture designation1 Learning Sheet|2004}}. The burial facility consists of a one-sided {{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} opening westward {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Inside the chamber are two house-shaped sarcophagi: one made of Aso welded tuff (Aso pink stone/Makado stone) and another made of {{ill|Futagoyama|ja|二上山|12=draft}} tuff. These sarcophagi, along with a variety of grave goods such as silver ear ornaments, iron weapons, and over 10,000 glass beads, were uncovered during excavations {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}{{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture designation1 Learning Sheet|2004}}. The construction period is estimated to be the early 6th century, placing it in the late Kofun period {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. It is believed to be the tomb of a regional leader and represents the first use of Aso welded tuff sarcophagi in the Yasu River basin {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. The burial goods reflect the power and influence of the entombed individual. The kofun site was Historic site a national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft}}} in 1941. It has since been preserved and opened to the public as part of the Sakuraba Historic Park, though access to the stone chamber is restricted and allowed only during annual autumn openings. == Archaeological History == * '''1941''': Designated as a national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft}}} (December 13) {{National Historic site Database|401|1579|Oiwayama Kofun Group}}. * '''1982''': Surveyed {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. * '''1985''': Six additional mounds were incorporated into the previously Historic site historic sites "Maruyama Kofun" and "Kozan Kofun," leading to the renaming of the Historic site as "Oiwayama Kofun Group" . * '''2001''': Excavations and maintenance reports by the Yasu Town Board of Education. * '''2001''': Sakuraba Historic Park opened on November 3 {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. == burial == [[File:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the burial chamber}}]] [[File:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the burial chamber}}]] The burial facility is a one-sided {{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} opening to the west {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Its area are as follows {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}{{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}: * Total length: 10.8 meters * Chamber: Length 4.3 meters, width 2.4 meters, height 3.1 meters * Passage: Length 6.5 meters, width 1.4 meters The passage descends approximately 1 meter from the entrance to the chamber floor, with the ceiling stones also stepping down {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The chamber floor is paved with pebbles {{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture designation1 Learning Sheet|2004}}. Inside the chamber are two sarcophagi: * '''Hollowed-out house-shaped sarcophagus''': Located near the front of the chamber and presumed to be the primary burial. Made of Aso welded tuff (Aso pink stone/Makado stone), it measures 2.85 meters in length, 1.46 meters in width, and 1.83 meters in height. The lid stone has rope-loop projections and is painted with red pigment {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. * '''Assembled house-shaped sarcophagus''': Positioned at the back of the chamber, made of Futagoyama tuff. Its lid and front stones are damaged, but the base stone measures 2.45 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width, while the sarcophagus body is about 0.8 meters in height. This is considered one of the oldest examples of Futagoyama tuff sarcophagi in Shiga Prefecture {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), genshitsu-2.jpg|Chamber (facing rear wall) Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), sendou-2.jpg|Passage (facing entrance) Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), sendou-1.jpg|Passage (facing chamber) == burial goods == Notable grave goods recovered include {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}: * '''Ornaments''' ** Silver ear ornaments with pendants ** Silver spiral earrings ** Step pendants ** Silver hollow beads ** ~10,000 glass beads {{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture designation1 Learning Sheet|2004}} * '''Weapons''' ** Lamellar armor ** Helmet with horizontal riveting ** Sword with twisted ring pommel ** Silver spiral fittings ** Small knife with antler handle ** Over 10 iron spearheads ** Over 500 iron arrowheads ** Quiver * '''Horse gear''' ** Saddle fittings ** Stirrups ** Bits == Related Facilities == * Sakuraba Historic Park Intypeation Center (Yasu City, Koshinohara) * Yasu City Museum of History and Folklore (Dotaku Museum, Yasu City, Tsuji Town) == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{PDFlink|[https://www.yasu-kankou.com/common/files/pamph_07.pdf Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet]}} (Yasu City Board of Education, designation1 Protection Section, Sakuraba Historic Park Intypeation Center, linked from Yasu City Tourism Association) {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. * Explanation boards (typeer Yasu Town Board of Education, 2001). * Prefectural publications: ** {{PDFlink|[http://www.shiga-ec.ed.jp/www/contents/1438304524592/html/common/other/55d173d3025.pdf "Oiwayama Kofun Group"]}} Shiga Prefectural Board of Education, designation1 Protection Section, 2004. * Encyclopedias: ** {{Cite book|title=Maruyama Kofun|series=Continued Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Shuppan|year=2002}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōiwayama Kofun Cluster|ja|大岩山古墳群|12=draft}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that It has a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} type and is one of the Kofuns that comprise the {{ill|Ōiwayama Kofun Cluster|ja|大岩山古墳群|12=draft}}, Historic site as a national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft}}}. == Overview == This kofun was constructed at the northern tip of a hill extending from Oiwayama to the right bank of the Yasu River in southern Shiga Prefecture. While the mound has been subject to looting, recent excavations were conducted as part of historic site maintenance efforts. The mound is circular, measuring 28 meters in diameter and approximately 8 meters in height {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}{{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. Initially thought to be a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft}}, it is now recognized as a round kofun {{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture designation1 Learning Sheet|2004}}. The burial facility consists of a one-sided {{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} opening westward {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Inside the chamber are two house-shaped sarcophagi: one made of Aso welded tuff (Aso pink stone/Makado stone) and another made of {{ill|Futagoyama|ja|二上山|12=draft}} tuff. These sarcophagi, along with a variety of grave goods such as silver ear ornaments, iron weapons, and over 10,000 glass beads, were uncovered during excavations {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}{{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture designation1 Learning Sheet|2004}}. The construction period is estimated to be the early 6th century, placing it in the late Kofun period {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. It is believed to be the tomb of a regional leader and represents the first use of Aso welded tuff sarcophagi in the Yasu River basin {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. The burial goods reflect the power and influence of the entombed individual. The kofun site was Historic site a national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft}}} in 1941. It has since been preserved and opened to the public as part of the Sakuraba Historic Park, though access to the stone chamber is restricted and allowed only during annual autumn openings. == Archaeological History == * '''1941''': Designated as a national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft}}} (December 13) {{National Historic site Database|401|1579|Oiwayama Kofun Group}}. * '''1982''': Surveyed {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. * '''1985''': Six additional mounds were incorporated into the previously Historic site historic sites "Maruyama Kofun" and "Kozan Kofun," leading to the renaming of the Historic site as "Oiwayama Kofun Group" . * '''2001''': Excavations and maintenance reports by the Yasu Town Board of Education. * '''2001''': Sakuraba Historic Park opened on November 3 {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. == burial == [[File:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the burial chamber}}]] [[File:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the burial chamber}}]] The burial facility is a one-sided {{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} opening to the west {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Its area are as follows {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}{{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}: * Total length: 10.8 meters * Chamber: Length 4.3 meters, width 2.4 meters, height 3.1 meters * Passage: Length 6.5 meters, width 1.4 meters The passage descends approximately 1 meter from the entrance to the chamber floor, with the ceiling stones also stepping down {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The chamber floor is paved with pebbles {{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture designation1 Learning Sheet|2004}}. Inside the chamber are two sarcophagi: * '''Hollowed-out house-shaped sarcophagus''': Located near the front of the chamber and presumed to be the primary burial. Made of Aso welded tuff (Aso pink stone/Makado stone), it measures 2.85 meters in length, 1.46 meters in width, and 1.83 meters in height. The lid stone has rope-loop projections and is painted with red pigment {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. * '''Assembled house-shaped sarcophagus''': Positioned at the back of the chamber, made of Futagoyama tuff. Its lid and front stones are damaged, but the base stone measures 2.45 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width, while the sarcophagus body is about 0.8 meters in height. This is considered one of the oldest examples of Futagoyama tuff sarcophagi in Shiga Prefecture {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. It has since been preserved and opened to the public as part of the Sakuraba Historic Park, though access to the stone chamber is restricted and allowed only during annual autumn openings. == Archaeological History == * '''1941''': Designated as a national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft}}} (December 13) {{National Historic site Database|401|1579|Oiwayama Kofun Group}}. * '''1982''': Surveyed {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. * '''1985''': Six additional mounds were incorporated into the previously Historic site historic sites "Maruyama Kofun" and "Kozan Kofun," leading to the renaming of the Historic site as "Oiwayama Kofun Group" . * '''2001''': Excavations and maintenance reports by the Yasu Town Board of Education. * '''2001''': Sakuraba Historic Park opened on November 3 {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. == burial == [[File:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the burial chamber}}]] [[File:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the burial chamber}}]] The burial facility is a one-sided {{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} opening to the west {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Its area are as follows {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}{{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}: * Total length: 10.8 meters * Chamber: Length 4.3 meters, width 2.4 meters, height 3.1 meters * Passage: Length 6.5 meters, width 1.4 meters The passage descends approximately 1 meter from the entrance to the chamber floor, with the ceiling stones also stepping down {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The chamber floor is paved with pebbles {{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture designation1 Learning Sheet|2004}}. Inside the chamber are two sarcophagi: * '''Hollowed-out house-shaped sarcophagus''': Located near the front of the chamber and presumed to be the primary burial. Made of Aso welded tuff (Aso pink stone/Makado stone), it measures 2.85 meters in length, 1.46 meters in width, and 1.83 meters in height. The lid stone has rope-loop projections and is painted with red pigment {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. * '''Assembled house-shaped sarcophagus''': Positioned at the back of the chamber, made of Futagoyama tuff. Its lid and front stones are damaged, but the base stone measures 2.45 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width, while the sarcophagus body is about 0.8 meters in height. This is considered one of the oldest examples of Futagoyama tuff sarcophagi in Shiga Prefecture {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. goods == Notable grave goods recovered include {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}: * '''Ornaments''' ** Silver ear ornaments with pendants ** Silver spiral earrings ** Step pendants ** Silver hollow beads ** ~10,000 glass beads {{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture designation1 Learning Sheet|2004}} * '''Weapons''' ** Lamellar armor ** Helmet with horizontal riveting ** Sword with twisted ring pommel ** Silver spiral fittings ** Small knife with antler handle ** Over 10 iron spearheads ** Over 500 iron arrowheads ** Quiver * '''Horse gear''' ** Saddle fittings ** Stirrups ** {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} |name=Maruyama Kofun |image=[[File:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), entrance.jpg|280px]]
Mound and stone chamber entrance |Other_name= | part_of={{ill|Oiwayama Kofun Group|ja|大岩山古墳群|12=draft}} |location={{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|12=draft}}{{ill|Yasu|ja|野洲市|12=draft}} Koshinohara (Azamaruyama)
(Sakuraba Historic Park) |Lat_deg=35|Lat_min=4|Lat_sec=26.82 |Lon_deg=136|Lon_min=2|Lon_sec=28.35 |omitted=JP-25 |type={{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} |map_size=Diameter 28m
Height 8m |burial=One-sided {{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}
(Two house-shaped sarcophagi inside) |burial goods=Numerous grave goods |Accompanying_mounds= |built=Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft}} (or early 6th century) |Interred_individual= |Mausoleum= |Cultural_property=Included in the national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft}}} "Oiwayama Kofun Group" |Tangible_notes= |Special_notes= |map=Japan Shiga |type=円墳 |map_dot_label=Maruyama Kofun |map_dot_label=bottom }} '''Maruyama Kofun''' (円山古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Yasu|ja|野洲市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|12=draft}}, in the Koshinohara area. It has a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} type and is one of the Kofuns that comprise the {{ill|Ōiwayama Kofun Cluster|ja|大岩山古墳群|12=draft}}, Historic site as a national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft}}}. == Overview == This kofun was constructed at the northern tip of a hill extending from Oiwayama to the right bank of the Yasu River in southern Shiga Prefecture. While the mound has been subject to looting, recent excavations were conducted as part of historic site maintenance efforts. The mound is circular, measuring 28 meters in diameter and approximately 8 meters in height {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}{{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. Initially thought to be a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft}}, it is now recognized as a round kofun {{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture designation1 Learning Sheet|2004}}. The burial facility consists of a one-sided {{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} opening westward {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Inside the chamber are two house-shaped sarcophagi: one made of Aso welded tuff (Aso pink stone/Makado stone) and another made of {{ill|Futagoyama|ja|二上山|12=draft}} tuff. These sarcophagi, along with a variety of grave goods such as silver ear ornaments, iron weapons, and over 10,000 glass beads, were uncovered during excavations {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}{{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture designation1 Learning Sheet|2004}}. The construction period is estimated to be the early 6th century, placing it in the late Kofun period {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. It is believed to be the tomb of a regional leader and represents the first use of Aso welded tuff sarcophagi in the Yasu River basin {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. The burial goods reflect the power and influence of the entombed individual. The kofun site was Historic site a national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft}}} in 1941. It has since been preserved and opened to the public as part of the Sakuraba Historic Park, though access to the stone chamber is restricted and allowed only during annual autumn openings. == Archaeological History == * '''1941''': Designated as a national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft}}} (December 13) {{National Historic site Database|401|1579|Oiwayama Kofun Group}}. * '''1982''': Surveyed {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. * '''1985''': Six additional mounds were incorporated into the previously Historic site historic sites "Maruyama Kofun" and "Kozan Kofun," leading to the renaming of the Historic site as "Oiwayama Kofun Group" . * '''2001''': Excavations and maintenance reports by the Yasu Town Board of Education. * '''2001''': Sakuraba Historic Park opened on November 3 {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. == burial == [[File:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the burial chamber}}]] [[File:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the burial chamber}}]] The burial facility is a one-sided {{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} opening to the west {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Its area are as follows {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}{{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}: * Total length: 10.8 meters * Chamber: Length 4.3 meters, width 2.4 meters, height 3.1 meters * Passage: Length 6.5 meters, width 1.4 meters The passage descends approximately 1 meter from the entrance to the chamber floor, with the ceiling stones also stepping down {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The chamber floor is paved with pebbles {{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture designation1 Learning Sheet|2004}}. Inside the chamber are two sarcophagi: * '''Hollowed-out house-shaped sarcophagus''': Located near the front of the chamber and presumed to be the primary burial. Made of Aso welded tuff (Aso pink stone/Makado stone), it measures 2.85 meters in length, 1.46 meters in width, and 1.83 meters in height. The lid stone has rope-loop projections and is painted with red pigment {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. * '''Assembled house-shaped sarcophagus''': Positioned at the back of the chamber, made of Futagoyama tuff. Its lid and front stones are damaged, but the base stone measures 2.45 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width, while the sarcophagus body is about 0.8 meters in height. This is considered one of the oldest examples of Futagoyama tuff sarcophagi in Shiga Prefecture {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} |name=Maruyama Kofun |image=[[File:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), entrance.jpg|280px]]
Mound and stone chamber entrance |Other_name= | part_of={{ill|Oiwayama Kofun Group|ja|大岩山古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} |location={{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Yasu|ja|野洲市|12=draft|12=draft}} Koshinohara (Azamaruyama)
(Sakuraba Historic Park) |Lat_deg=35|Lat_min=4|Lat_sec=26.82 |Lon_deg=136|Lon_min=2|Lon_sec=28.35 |omitted=JP-25 |type={{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} |map_size=Diameter 28m
Height 8m |burial=One-sided {{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}
(Two house-shaped sarcophagi inside) |burial goods=Numerous grave goods |Accompanying_mounds= |built=Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft|12=draft}} (or early 6th century) |Interred_individual= |Mausoleum= |Cultural_property=Included in the national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft|12=draft}}} "Oiwayama Kofun Group" |Tangible_notes= |Special_notes= |map=Japan Shiga |type=円墳 |map_dot_label=Maruyama Kofun |map_dot_label=bottom }} '''Maruyama Kofun''' (円山古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Yasu|ja|野洲市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|12=draft|12=draft}}, in the Koshinohara area. It has a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} type and is one of the Kofuns that comprise the {{ill|Ōiwayama Kofun Cluster|ja|大岩山古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}, Historic site as a national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft|12=draft}}}. == Overview == This kofun was constructed at the northern tip of a hill extending from Oiwayama to the right bank of the Yasu River in southern Shiga Prefecture. While the mound has been subject to looting, recent excavations were conducted as part of historic site maintenance efforts. The mound is circular, measuring 28 meters in diameter and approximately 8 meters in height {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}{{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. Initially thought to be a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}, it is now recognized as a round kofun {{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture designation1 Learning Sheet|2004}}. The burial facility consists of a one-sided {{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} opening westward {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Inside the chamber are two house-shaped sarcophagi: one made of Aso welded tuff (Aso pink stone/Makado stone) and another made of {{ill|Futagoyama|ja|二上山|12=draft|12=draft}} tuff. These sarcophagi, along with a variety of grave goods such as silver ear ornaments, iron weapons, and over 10,000 glass beads, were uncovered during excavations {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}{{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture designation1 Learning Sheet|2004}}. The construction period is estimated to be the early 6th century, placing it in the late Kofun period {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. It is believed to be the tomb of a regional leader and represents the first use of Aso welded tuff sarcophagi in the Yasu River basin {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. The burial goods reflect the power and influence of the entombed individual. The kofun site was Historic site a national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft|12=draft}}} in 1941. It has since been preserved and opened to the public as part of the Sakuraba Historic Park, though access to the stone chamber is restricted and allowed only during annual autumn openings. == Archaeological History == * '''1941''': Designated as a national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft|12=draft}}} (December 13) {{National Historic site Database|401|1579|Oiwayama Kofun Group}}. * '''1982''': Surveyed {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. * '''1985''': Six additional mounds were incorporated into the previously Historic site historic sites "Maruyama Kofun" and "Kozan Kofun," leading to the renaming of the Historic site as "Oiwayama Kofun Group" . * '''2001''': Excavations and maintenance reports by the Yasu Town Board of Education. * '''2001''': Sakuraba Historic Park opened on November 3 {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. == burial == [[File:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the burial chamber}}]] [[File:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the burial chamber}}]] The burial facility is a one-sided {{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} opening to the west {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Its area are as follows {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}{{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}: * Total length: 10.8 meters * Chamber: Length 4.3 meters, width 2.4 meters, height 3.1 meters * Passage: Length 6.5 meters, width 1.4 meters The passage descends approximately 1 meter from the entrance to the chamber floor, with the ceiling stones also stepping down {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The chamber floor is paved with pebbles {{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture designation1 Learning Sheet|2004}}. Inside the chamber are two sarcophagi: * '''Hollowed-out house-shaped sarcophagus''': Located near the front of the chamber and presumed to be the primary burial. Made of Aso welded tuff (Aso pink stone/Makado stone), it measures 2.85 meters in length, 1.46 meters in width, and 1.83 meters in height. The lid stone has rope-loop projections and is painted with red pigment {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. * '''Assembled house-shaped sarcophagus''': Positioned at the back of the chamber, made of Futagoyama tuff. Its lid and front stones are damaged, but the base stone measures 2.45 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width, while the sarcophagus body is about 0.8 meters in height. This is considered one of the oldest examples of Futagoyama tuff sarcophagi in Shiga Prefecture {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), genshitsu-2.jpg|Chamber (facing rear wall) Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), sendou-2.jpg|Passage (facing entrance) Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), sendou-1.jpg|Passage (facing chamber) == burial goods == Notable grave goods recovered include {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}: * '''Ornaments''' ** Silver ear ornaments with pendants ** Silver spiral earrings ** Step pendants ** Silver hollow beads ** ~10,000 glass beads {{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture designation1 Learning Sheet|2004}} * '''Weapons''' ** Lamellar armor ** Helmet with horizontal riveting ** Sword with twisted ring pommel ** Silver spiral fittings ** Small knife with antler handle ** Over 10 iron spearheads ** Over 500 iron arrowheads ** Quiver * '''Horse gear''' ** Saddle fittings ** Stirrups ** Bits == Related Facilities == * Sakuraba Historic Park Intypeation Center (Yasu City, Koshinohara) * Yasu City Museum of History and Folklore (Dotaku Museum, Yasu City, Tsuji Town) == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{PDFlink|[https://www.yasu-kankou.com/common/files/pamph_07.pdf Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet]}} (Yasu City Board of Education, designation1 Protection Section, Sakuraba Historic Park Intypeation Center, linked from Yasu City Tourism Association) {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. * Explanation boards (typeer Yasu Town Board of Education, 2001). * Prefectural publications: ** {{PDFlink|[http://www.shiga-ec.ed.jp/www/contents/1438304524592/html/common/other/55d173d3025.pdf "Oiwayama Kofun Group"]}} Shiga Prefectural Board of Education, designation1 Protection Section, 2004. * Encyclopedias: ** {{Cite book|title=Maruyama Kofun|series=Continued Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Shuppan|year=2002}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōiwayama Kofun Cluster|ja|大岩山古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Maruyama Kofun (Yasu)}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yasu]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:National Historic sites of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu)]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu)]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu)]] [[ja:円山古墳 (野洲市)]] [[sn:aruyama Kofun (Yasu)]]

(Two house-shaped sarcophagi inside) |burial goods=Numerous grave goods |Accompanying_mounds= |built=Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft|12=draft}} (or early 6th century) |Interred_individual= |Mausoleum= |Cultural_property=Included in the national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft|12=draft}}} "Oiwayama Kofun Group" |Tangible_notes= |Special_notes= |map=Japan Shiga |type=円墳 |map_dot_label=Maruyama Kofun |map_dot_label=bottom }} '''Maruyama Kofun''' (円山古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Yasu|ja|野洲市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|12=draft|12=draft}}, in the Koshinohara area. It has a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} type and is one of the Kofuns that comprise the {{ill|Ōiwayama Kofun Cluster|ja|大岩山古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}, Historic site as a national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft|12=draft}}}. == Overview == This kofun was constructed at the northern tip of a hill extending from Oiwayama to the right bank of the Yasu River in southern Shiga Prefecture. While the mound has been subject to looting, recent excavations were conducted as part of historic site maintenance efforts. The mound is circular, measuring 28 meters in diameter and approximately 8 meters in height {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}{{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. Initially thought to be a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}, it is now recognized as a round kofun {{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture designation1 Learning Sheet|2004}}. The burial facility consists of a one-sided {{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} opening westward {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Inside the chamber are two house-shaped sarcophagi: one made of Aso welded tuff (Aso pink stone/Makado stone) and another made of {{ill|Futagoyama|ja|二上山|12=draft|12=draft}} tuff. These sarcophagi, along with a variety of grave goods such as silver ear ornaments, iron weapons, and over 10,000 glass beads, were uncovered during excavations {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}{{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture designation1 Learning Sheet|2004}}. The construction period is estimated to be the early 6th century, placing it in the late Kofun period {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. It is believed to be the tomb of a regional leader and represents the first use of Aso welded tuff sarcophagi in the Yasu River basin {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. The burial goods reflect the power and influence of the entombed individual. The kofun site was Historic site a national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft|12=draft}}} in 1941. It has since been preserved and opened to the public as part of the Sakuraba Historic Park, though access to the stone chamber is restricted and allowed only during annual autumn openings. == Archaeological History == * '''1941''': Designated as a national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft|12=draft}}} (December 13) {{National Historic site Database|401|1579|Oiwayama Kofun Group}}. * '''1982''': Surveyed {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. * '''1985''': Six additional mounds were incorporated into the previously Historic site historic sites "Maruyama Kofun" and "Kozan Kofun," leading to the renaming of the Historic site as "Oiwayama Kofun Group" . * '''2001''': Excavations and maintenance reports by the Yasu Town Board of Education. * '''2001''': Sakuraba Historic Park opened on November 3 {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. == burial == [[File:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the burial chamber}}]] [[File:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the burial chamber}}]] The burial facility is a one-sided {{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} opening to the west {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Its area are as follows {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}{{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}: * Total length: 10.8 meters * Chamber: Length 4.3 meters, width 2.4 meters, height 3.1 meters * Passage: Length 6.5 meters, width 1.4 meters The passage descends approximately 1 meter from the entrance to the chamber floor, with the ceiling stones also stepping down {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The chamber floor is paved with pebbles {{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture designation1 Learning Sheet|2004}}. Inside the chamber are two sarcophagi: * '''Hollowed-out house-shaped sarcophagus''': Located near the front of the chamber and presumed to be the primary burial. Made of Aso welded tuff (Aso pink stone/Makado stone), it measures 2.85 meters in length, 1.46 meters in width, and 1.83 meters in height. The lid stone has rope-loop projections and is painted with red pigment {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. * '''Assembled house-shaped sarcophagus''': Positioned at the back of the chamber, made of Futagoyama tuff. Its lid and front stones are damaged, but the base stone measures 2.45 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width, while the sarcophagus body is about 0.8 meters in height. This is considered one of the oldest examples of Futagoyama tuff sarcophagi in Shiga Prefecture {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), genshitsu-2.jpg|Chamber (facing rear wall) Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), sendou-2.jpg|Passage (facing entrance) Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), sendou-1.jpg|Passage (facing chamber) == burial goods == Notable grave goods recovered include {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}: * '''Ornaments''' ** Silver ear ornaments with pendants ** Silver spiral earrings ** Step pendants ** Silver hollow beads ** ~10,000 glass beads {{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture designation1 Learning Sheet|2004}} * '''Weapons''' ** Lamellar armor ** Helmet with horizontal riveting ** Sword with twisted ring pommel ** Silver spiral fittings ** Small knife with antler handle ** Over 10 iron spearheads ** Over 500 iron arrowheads ** Quiver * '''Horse gear''' ** Saddle fittings ** Stirrups ** Bits == Related Facilities == * Sakuraba Historic Park Intypeation Center (Yasu City, Koshinohara) * Yasu City Museum of History and Folklore (Dotaku Museum, Yasu City, Tsuji Town) == References == Sakuraba Historic Park Intypeation Center, linked from Yasu City Tourism Association) {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. * Explanation boards (typeer Yasu Town Board of Education, 2001). * Prefectural publications: ** {{PDFlink|[http://www.shiga-ec.ed.jp/www/contents/1438304524592/html/common/other/55d173d3025.pdf "Oiwayama Kofun Group"]}} Shiga Prefectural Board of Education, designation1 Protection Section, 2004. * Encyclopedias: ** {{Cite book|title=Maruyama Kofun|series=Continued Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Shuppan|year=2002}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōiwayama Kofun Cluster|ja|大岩山古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Maruyama Kofun (Yasu)}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu)]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu)]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu)]] [[ja:円山古墳 (野洲市)]] While the mound has been subject to looting, recent excavations were conducted as part of historic site maintenance efforts. The mound is circular, measuring 28 meters in diameter and approximately 8 meters in height {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}{{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. Inside the chamber are two house-shaped sarcophagi: one made of Aso welded tuff (Aso pink stone/Makado stone) and another made of {{ill|Futagoyama|ja|二上山|12=draft|12=draft}} tuff. It is believed to be the tomb of a regional leader and represents the first use of Aso welded tuff sarcophagi in the Yasu River basin {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. The burial goods reflect the power and influence of the entombed individual. The kofun site was Historic site a national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft|12=draft}}} in 1941. It has since been preserved and opened to the public as part of the Sakuraba Historic Park, though access to the stone chamber is restricted and allowed only during annual autumn openings. == Archaeological History == * '''1941''': Designated as a national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft|12=draft}}} (December 13) {{National Historic site Database|401|1579|Oiwayama Kofun Group}}. * '''1982''': Surveyed {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. * '''1985''': Six additional mounds were incorporated into the previously Historic site historic sites "Maruyama Kofun" and "Kozan Kofun," leading to the renaming of the Historic site as "Oiwayama Kofun Group" . * '''2001''': Excavations and maintenance reports by the Yasu Town Board of Education. * '''2001''': Sakuraba Historic Park opened on November 3 {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. == burial == [[File:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the burial chamber}}]] [[File:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the burial chamber}}]] The burial facility is a one-sided {{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} opening to the west {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Its area are as follows {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}{{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}: * Total length: 10.8 meters * Chamber: Length 4.3 meters, width 2.4 meters, height 3.1 meters * Passage: Length 6.5 meters, width 1.4 meters The passage descends approximately 1 meter from the entrance to the chamber floor, with the ceiling stones also stepping down {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The chamber floor is paved with pebbles {{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture designation1 Learning Sheet|2004}}. Inside the chamber are two sarcophagi: * '''Hollowed-out house-shaped sarcophagus''': Located near the front of the chamber and presumed to be the primary burial. Made of Aso welded tuff (Aso pink stone/Makado stone), it measures 2.85 meters in length, 1.46 meters in width, and 1.83 meters in height. The lid stone has rope-loop projections and is painted with red pigment {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. * '''Assembled house-shaped sarcophagus''': Positioned at the back of the chamber, made of Futagoyama tuff. Its lid and front stones are damaged, but the base stone measures 2.45 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width, while the sarcophagus body is about 0.8 meters in height. This is considered one of the oldest examples of Futagoyama tuff sarcophagi in Shiga Prefecture {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The burial goods reflect the power and influence of the entombed individual. The kofun site was Historic site a national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft|12=draft}}} in 1941. It has since been preserved and opened to the public as part of the Sakuraba Historic Park, though access to the stone chamber is restricted and allowed only during annual autumn openings. == Archaeological History == * '''1941''': Designated as a national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft|12=draft}}} (December 13) {{National Historic site Database|401|1579|Oiwayama Kofun Group}}. * '''1982''': Surveyed {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. * '''1985''': Six additional mounds were incorporated into the previously Historic site historic sites "Maruyama Kofun" and "Kozan Kofun," leading to the renaming of the Historic site as "Oiwayama Kofun Group" . * '''2001''': Excavations and maintenance reports by the Yasu Town Board of Education. * '''2001''': Sakuraba Historic Park opened on November 3 {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. == burial == [[File:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the burial chamber}}]] [[File:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the burial chamber}}]] The burial facility is a one-sided {{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} opening to the west {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Its area are as follows {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}{{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}: * Total length: 10.8 meters * Chamber: Length 4.3 meters, width 2.4 meters, height 3.1 meters * Passage: Length 6.5 meters, width 1.4 meters The passage descends approximately 1 meter from the entrance to the chamber floor, with the ceiling stones also stepping down {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The chamber floor is paved with pebbles {{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture designation1 Learning Sheet|2004}}. Inside the chamber are two sarcophagi: * '''Hollowed-out house-shaped sarcophagus''': Located near the front of the chamber and presumed to be the primary burial. Made of Aso welded tuff (Aso pink stone/Makado stone), it measures 2.85 meters in length, 1.46 meters in width, and 1.83 meters in height. The lid stone has rope-loop projections and is painted with red pigment {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. * '''Assembled house-shaped sarcophagus''': Positioned at the back of the chamber, made of Futagoyama tuff. Its lid and front stones are damaged, but the base stone measures 2.45 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width, while the sarcophagus body is about 0.8 meters in height. This is considered one of the oldest examples of Futagoyama tuff sarcophagi in Shiga Prefecture {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), genshitsu-2.jpg|Chamber (facing rear wall) Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), sendou-2.jpg|Passage (facing entrance) Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), sendou-1.jpg|Passage (facing chamber) == burial goods == Notable grave goods recovered include {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}: * '''Ornaments''' ** Silver ear ornaments with pendants ** Silver spiral earrings ** Step pendants ** Silver hollow beads ** ~10,000 glass beads {{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture designation1 Learning Sheet|2004}} * '''Weapons''' ** Lamellar armor ** Helmet with horizontal riveting ** Sword with twisted ring pommel ** Silver spiral fittings ** Small knife with antler handle ** Over 10 iron spearheads ** Over 500 iron arrowheads ** Quiver * '''Horse gear''' ** Saddle fittings ** Stirrups ** Bits == Related Facilities == * Sakuraba Historic Park Intypeation Center (Yasu City, Koshinohara) * Yasu City Museum of History and Folklore (Dotaku Museum, Yasu City, Tsuji Town) == References == Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. Mound and stone chamber entrance |Other_name= | part_of={{ill|Oiwayama Kofun Group|ja|大岩山古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} |location={{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Yasu|ja|野洲市|12=draft|12=draft}} Koshinohara (Azamaruyama)
(Sakuraba Historic Park) |Lat_deg=35|Lat_min=4|Lat_sec=26.82 |Lon_deg=136|Lon_min=2|Lon_sec=28.35 |omitted=JP-25 |type={{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} |map_size=Diameter 28m
Height 8m |burial=One-sided {{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}
(Two house-shaped sarcophagi inside) |burial goods=Numerous grave goods |Accompanying_mounds= |built=Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft|12=draft}} (or early 6th century) |Interred_individual= |Mausoleum= |Cultural_property=Included in the national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft|12=draft}}} "Oiwayama Kofun Group" |Tangible_notes= |Special_notes= |map=Japan Shiga |type=円墳 |map_dot_label=Maruyama Kofun |map_dot_label=bottom }} '''Maruyama Kofun''' (円山古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Yasu|ja|野洲市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|12=draft|12=draft}}, in the Koshinohara area. It has a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} type and is one of the Kofuns that comprise the {{ill|Ōiwayama Kofun Cluster|ja|大岩山古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}, Historic site as a national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft|12=draft}}}. == Overview == This kofun was constructed at the northern tip of a hill extending from Oiwayama to the right bank of the Yasu River in southern Shiga Prefecture. While the mound has been subject to looting, recent excavations were conducted as part of historic site maintenance efforts. The mound is circular, measuring 28 meters in diameter and approximately 8 meters in height {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}{{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. Initially thought to be a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}, it is now recognized as a round kofun {{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture designation1 Learning Sheet|2004}}. The burial facility consists of a one-sided {{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} opening westward {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Inside the chamber are two house-shaped sarcophagi: one made of Aso welded tuff (Aso pink stone/Makado stone) and another made of {{ill|Futagoyama|ja|二上山|12=draft|12=draft}} tuff. These sarcophagi, along with a variety of grave goods such as silver ear ornaments, iron weapons, and over 10,000 glass beads, were uncovered during excavations {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}{{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture designation1 Learning Sheet|2004}}. The construction period is estimated to be the early 6th century, placing it in the late Kofun period {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. It is believed to be the tomb of a regional leader and represents the first use of Aso welded tuff sarcophagi in the Yasu River basin {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. The burial goods reflect the power and influence of the entombed individual. The kofun site was Historic site a national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft|12=draft}}} in 1941. It has since been preserved and opened to the public as part of the Sakuraba Historic Park, though access to the stone chamber is restricted and allowed only during annual autumn openings. == Archaeological History == * '''1941''': Designated as a national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft|12=draft}}} (December 13) {{National Historic site Database|401|1579|Oiwayama Kofun Group}}. * '''1982''': Surveyed {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. * '''1985''': Six additional mounds were incorporated into the previously Historic site historic sites "Maruyama Kofun" and "Kozan Kofun," leading to the renaming of the Historic site as "Oiwayama Kofun Group" . * '''2001''': Excavations and maintenance reports by the Yasu Town Board of Education. * '''2001''': Sakuraba Historic Park opened on November 3 {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. == burial == [[File:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the burial chamber}}]] [[File:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the burial chamber}}]] The burial facility is a one-sided {{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} opening to the west {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Its area are as follows {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}{{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}: * Total length: 10.8 meters * Chamber: Length 4.3 meters, width 2.4 meters, height 3.1 meters * Passage: Length 6.5 meters, width 1.4 meters The passage descends approximately 1 meter from the entrance to the chamber floor, with the ceiling stones also stepping down {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The chamber floor is paved with pebbles {{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture designation1 Learning Sheet|2004}}. Inside the chamber are two sarcophagi: * '''Hollowed-out house-shaped sarcophagus''': Located near the front of the chamber and presumed to be the primary burial. Made of Aso welded tuff (Aso pink stone/Makado stone), it measures 2.85 meters in length, 1.46 meters in width, and 1.83 meters in height. The lid stone has rope-loop projections and is painted with red pigment {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. * '''Assembled house-shaped sarcophagus''': Positioned at the back of the chamber, made of Futagoyama tuff. Its lid and front stones are damaged, but the base stone measures 2.45 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width, while the sarcophagus body is about 0.8 meters in height. This is considered one of the oldest examples of Futagoyama tuff sarcophagi in Shiga Prefecture {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. This is considered one of the oldest examples of Futagoyama tuff sarcophagi in Shiga Prefecture {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. goods == Notable grave goods recovered include {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}: * '''Ornaments''' ** Silver ear ornaments with pendants ** Silver spiral earrings ** Step pendants ** Silver hollow beads ** ~10,000 glass beads {{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture designation1 Learning Sheet|2004}} * '''Weapons''' ** Lamellar armor ** Helmet with horizontal riveting ** Sword with twisted ring pommel ** Silver spiral fittings ** Small knife with antler handle ** Over 10 iron spearheads ** Over 500 iron arrowheads ** Quiver * '''Horse gear''' ** Saddle fittings ** Stirrups ** {{afc comment|1=See [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} |name=Maruyama Kofun |image=[[File:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), entrance.jpg|280px]]
Mound and stone chamber entrance |Other_name= | part_of={{ill|Oiwayama Kofun Group|ja|大岩山古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} |location={{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Yasu|ja|野洲市|12=draft|12=draft}} Koshinohara (Azamaruyama)
(Sakuraba Historic Park) |Lat_deg=35|Lat_min=4|Lat_sec=26.82 |Lon_deg=136|Lon_min=2|Lon_sec=28.35 |omitted=JP-25 |type={{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} |map_size=Diameter 28m
Height 8m |burial=One-sided {{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}
(Two house-shaped sarcophagi inside) |burial goods=Numerous grave goods |Accompanying_mounds= |built=Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft|12=draft}} (or early 6th century) |Interred_individual= |Mausoleum= |Cultural_property=Included in the national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft|12=draft}}} "Oiwayama Kofun Group" |Tangible_notes= |Special_notes= |map=Japan Shiga |type=円墳 |map_dot_label=Maruyama Kofun |map_dot_label=bottom }} '''Maruyama Kofun''' (円山古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Yasu|ja|野洲市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|12=draft|12=draft}}, in the Koshinohara area. It has a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} type and is one of the Kofuns that comprise the {{ill|Ōiwayama Kofun Cluster|ja|大岩山古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}, Historic site as a national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft|12=draft}}}. == Overview == This kofun was constructed at the northern tip of a hill extending from Oiwayama to the right bank of the Yasu River in southern Shiga Prefecture. While the mound has been subject to looting, recent excavations were conducted as part of historic site maintenance efforts. The mound is circular, measuring 28 meters in diameter and approximately 8 meters in height {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}{{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. Initially thought to be a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}, it is now recognized as a round kofun {{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture designation1 Learning Sheet|2004}}. The burial facility consists of a one-sided {{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} opening westward {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Inside the chamber are two house-shaped sarcophagi: one made of Aso welded tuff (Aso pink stone/Makado stone) and another made of {{ill|Futagoyama|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|12=draft|12=draft}} tuff. These sarcophagi, along with a variety of grave goods such as silver ear ornaments, iron weapons, and over 10,000 glass beads, were uncovered during excavations {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}{{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture designation1 Learning Sheet|2004}}. The construction period is estimated to be the early 6th century, placing it in the late Kofun period {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. It is believed to be the tomb of a regional leader and represents the first use of Aso welded tuff sarcophagi in the Yasu River basin {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. The burial goods reflect the power and influence of the entombed individual. The kofun site was Historic site a national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft|12=draft}}} in 1941. It has since been preserved and opened to the public as part of the Sakuraba Historic Park, though access to the stone chamber is restricted and allowed only during annual autumn openings. == Archaeological History == * '''1941''': Designated as a national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft|12=draft}}} (December 13) {{National Historic site Database|401|1579|Oiwayama Kofun Group}}. * '''1982''': Surveyed {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. * '''1985''': Six additional mounds were incorporated into the previously Historic site historic sites "Maruyama Kofun" and "Kozan Kofun," leading to the renaming of the Historic site as "Oiwayama Kofun Group" . * '''2001''': Excavations and maintenance reports by the Yasu Town Board of Education. * '''2001''': Sakuraba Historic Park opened on November 3 {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. == burial == [[File:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the burial chamber}}]] [[File:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the burial chamber}}]] The burial facility is a one-sided {{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} opening to the west {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Its area are as follows {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}{{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}: * Total length: 10.8 meters * Chamber: Length 4.3 meters, width 2.4 meters, height 3.1 meters * Passage: Length 6.5 meters, width 1.4 meters The passage descends approximately 1 meter from the entrance to the chamber floor, with the ceiling stones also stepping down {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The chamber floor is paved with pebbles {{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture designation1 Learning Sheet|2004}}. Inside the chamber are two sarcophagi: * '''Hollowed-out house-shaped sarcophagus''': Located near the front of the chamber and presumed to be the primary burial. Made of Aso welded tuff (Aso pink stone/Makado stone), it measures 2.85 meters in length, 1.46 meters in width, and 1.83 meters in height. The lid stone has rope-loop projections and is painted with red pigment {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. * '''Assembled house-shaped sarcophagus''': Positioned at the back of the chamber, made of Futagoyama tuff. Its lid and front stones are damaged, but the base stone measures 2.45 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width, while the sarcophagus body is about 0.8 meters in height. This is considered one of the oldest examples of Futagoyama tuff sarcophagi in Shiga Prefecture {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), genshitsu-2.jpg|Chamber (facing rear wall) Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), sendou-2.jpg|Passage (facing entrance) Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), sendou-1.jpg|Passage (facing chamber) == burial goods == Notable grave goods recovered include {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}: * '''Ornaments''' ** Silver ear ornaments with pendants ** Silver spiral earrings ** Step pendants ** Silver hollow beads ** ~10,000 glass beads {{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture designation1 Learning Sheet|2004}} * '''Weapons''' ** Lamellar armor ** Helmet with horizontal riveting ** Sword with twisted ring pommel ** Silver spiral fittings ** Small knife with antler handle ** Over 10 iron spearheads ** Over 500 iron arrowheads ** Quiver * '''Horse gear''' ** Saddle fittings ** Stirrups ** Bits == Related Facilities == * Sakuraba Historic Park Intypeation Center (Yasu City, Koshinohara) * Yasu City Museum of History and Folklore (Dotaku Museum, Yasu City, Tsuji Town) == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{PDFlink|[https://www.yasu-kankou.com/common/files/pamph_07.pdf Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet]}} (Yasu City Board of Education, designation1 Protection Section, Sakuraba Historic Park Intypeation Center, linked from Yasu City Tourism Association) {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. * Explanation boards (typeer Yasu Town Board of Education, 2001). * Prefectural publications: ** {{PDFlink|[http://www.shiga-ec.ed.jp/www/contents/1438304524592/html/common/other/55d173d3025.pdf "Oiwayama Kofun Group"]}} Shiga Prefectural Board of Education, designation1 Protection Section, 2004. * Encyclopedias: ** {{Cite book|title=Maruyama Kofun|series=Continued Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Shuppan|year=2002}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōiwayama Kofun Cluster|ja|大岩山古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Maruyama Kofun (Yasu)}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yasu]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:National Historic sites of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu)]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown Made of Aso welded tuff (Aso pink stone/Makado stone), it measures 2.85 meters in length, 1.46 meters in width, and 1.83 meters in height. The lid stone has rope-loop projections and is painted with red pigment {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. * '''Assembled house-shaped sarcophagus''': Positioned at the back of the chamber, made of Futagoyama tuff. Its lid and front stones are damaged, but the base stone measures 2.45 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width, while the sarcophagus body is about 0.8 meters in height. This is considered one of the oldest examples of Futagoyama tuff sarcophagi in Shiga Prefecture {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}.
