Height 8m |burial=One-sided {{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}
(Two house-shaped sarcophagi inside) |burial goods=Numerous grave goods |Accompanying_mounds= |built=Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft|12=draft}} (or early 6th century) |Interred_individual= |Mausoleum= |Cultural_property=Included in the national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft|12=draft}}} "Oiwayama Kofun Group" |Tangible_notes= |Special_notes= |map=Japan Shiga |type=円墳 |map_dot_label=Maruyama Kofun |map_dot_label=bottom }} '''Maruyama Kofun''' (円山古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Yasu|ja|野洲市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|12=draft|12=draft}}, in the Koshinohara area. It has a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} type and is one of the Kofuns that comprise the {{ill|Ōiwayama Kofun Cluster|ja|大岩山古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}, Historic site as a national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft|12=draft}}}. == Overview == This kofun was constructed at the northern tip of a hill extending from Oiwayama to the right bank of the Yasu River in southern Shiga Prefecture. While the mound has been subject to looting, recent excavations were conducted as part of historic site maintenance efforts. The mound is circular, measuring 28 meters in diameter and approximately 8 meters in height {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}{{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. Initially thought to be a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}, it is now recognized as a round kofun {{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture designation1 Learning Sheet|2004}}. The burial facility consists of a one-sided {{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} opening westward {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Inside the chamber are two house-shaped sarcophagi: one made of Aso welded tuff (Aso pink stone/Makado stone) and another made of {{ill|Futagoyama|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|12=draft|12=draft}} tuff. These sarcophagi, along with a variety of grave goods such as silver ear ornaments, iron weapons, and over 10,000 glass beads, were uncovered during excavations {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}{{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture designation1 Learning Sheet|2004}}. The construction period is estimated to be the early 6th century, placing it in the late Kofun period {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. It is believed to be the tomb of a regional leader and represents the first use of Aso welded tuff sarcophagi in the Yasu River basin {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. The burial goods reflect the power and influence of the entombed individual. The kofun site was Historic site a national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft|12=draft}}} in 1941. It has since been preserved and opened to the public as part of the Sakuraba Historic Park, though access to the stone chamber is restricted and allowed only during annual autumn openings. == Archaeological History == * '''1941''': Designated as a national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft|12=draft}}} (December 13) {{National Historic site Database|401|1579|Oiwayama Kofun Group}}. * '''1982''': Surveyed {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. * '''1985''': Six additional mounds were incorporated into the previously Historic site historic sites "Maruyama Kofun" and "Kozan Kofun," leading to the renaming of the Historic site as "Oiwayama Kofun Group" . * '''2001''': Excavations and maintenance reports by the Yasu Town Board of Education. * '''2001''': Sakuraba Historic Park opened on November 3 {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. == burial == [[File:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the burial chamber}}]] [[File:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the burial chamber}}]] The burial facility is a one-sided {{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} opening to the west {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Its area are as follows {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}{{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}: * Total length: 10.8 meters * Chamber: Length 4.3 meters, width 2.4 meters, height 3.1 meters * Passage: Length 6.5 meters, width 1.4 meters The passage descends approximately 1 meter from the entrance to the chamber floor, with the ceiling stones also stepping down {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The chamber floor is paved with pebbles {{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture designation1 Learning Sheet|2004}}. Inside the chamber are two sarcophagi: * '''Hollowed-out house-shaped sarcophagus''': Located near the front of the chamber and presumed to be the primary burial. Made of Aso welded tuff (Aso pink stone/Makado stone), it measures 2.85 meters in length, 1.46 meters in width, and 1.83 meters in height. The lid stone has rope-loop projections and is painted with red pigment {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. * '''Assembled house-shaped sarcophagus''': Positioned at the back of the chamber, made of Futagoyama tuff. Its lid and front stones are damaged, but the base stone measures 2.45 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width, while the sarcophagus body is about 0.8 meters in height. This is considered one of the oldest examples of Futagoyama tuff sarcophagi in Shiga Prefecture {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Its area are as follows {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}{{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}: * Total length: 10.8 meters * Chamber: Length 4.3 meters, width 2.4 meters, height 3.1 meters * Passage: Length 6.5 meters, width 1.4 meters The passage descends approximately 1 meter from the entrance to the chamber floor, with the ceiling stones also stepping down {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The chamber floor is paved with pebbles {{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture designation1 Learning Sheet|2004}}. Inside the chamber are two sarcophagi: * '''Hollowed-out house-shaped sarcophagus''': Located near the front of the chamber and presumed to be the primary burial. Made of Aso welded tuff (Aso pink stone/Makado stone), it measures 2.85 meters in length, 1.46 meters in width, and 1.83 meters in height. The lid stone has rope-loop projections and is painted with red pigment {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. * '''Assembled house-shaped sarcophagus''': Positioned at the back of the chamber, made of Futagoyama tuff. Its lid and front stones are damaged, but the base stone measures 2.45 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width, while the sarcophagus body is about 0.8 meters in height. This is considered one of the oldest examples of Futagoyama tuff sarcophagi in Shiga Prefecture {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), genshitsu-2.jpg|Chamber (facing rear wall) Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), sendou-2.jpg|Passage (facing entrance) Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), sendou-1.jpg|Passage (facing chamber) == burial goods == Notable grave goods recovered include {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}: * '''Ornaments''' ** Silver ear ornaments with pendants ** Silver spiral earrings ** Step pendants ** Silver hollow beads ** ~10,000 glass beads {{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture designation1 Learning Sheet|2004}} * '''Weapons''' ** Lamellar armor ** Helmet with horizontal riveting ** Sword with twisted ring pommel ** Silver spiral fittings ** Small knife with antler handle ** Over 10 iron spearheads ** Over 500 iron arrowheads ** Quiver * '''Horse gear''' ** Saddle fittings ** Stirrups ** Bits == Related Facilities == * Sakuraba Historic Park Intypeation Center (Yasu City, Koshinohara) * Yasu City Museum of History and Folklore (Dotaku Museum, Yasu City, Tsuji Town) == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{PDFlink|[https://www.yasu-kankou.com/common/files/pamph_07.pdf Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet]}} (Yasu City Board of Education, designation1 Protection Section, Sakuraba Historic Park Intypeation Center, linked from Yasu City Tourism Association) {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. * Explanation boards (typeer Yasu Town Board of Education, 2001). * Prefectural publications: ** {{PDFlink|[http://www.shiga-ec.ed.jp/www/contents/1438304524592/html/common/other/55d173d3025.pdf "Oiwayama Kofun Group"]}} Shiga Prefectural Board of Education, designation1 Protection Section, 2004. * Encyclopedias: ** {{Cite book|title=Maruyama Kofun|series=Continued Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Shuppan|year=2002}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōiwayama Kofun Cluster|ja|大岩山古墳群}} {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Maruyama Kofun (Yasu)}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu)]][[Category:qq]] }} revision.comment: 14 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:円山古墳 (野洲市) up to rev 101832160
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 円山古墳 |画像 = [[File:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), entrance.jpg|280px]]
墳丘・石室開口部 |別名 = |所属 = [[大岩山古墳群]] |所在地 = [[滋賀県]][[野洲市]]小篠原（字円山）
（桜生史跡公園内） |緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 4|緯度秒 = 26.82 |経度度 =136|経度分 = 2|経度秒 = 28.35 |ISO = JP-25 |形状 = [[円墳]] |規模 = 直径28m
高さ8m |埋葬施設 = 片袖式[[横穴式石室]]
（内部に家形石棺2基） |出土品 = 副葬品多数 |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]前半（または6世紀初頭） |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 国の[[史跡]]「大岩山古墳群」に包含 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Shiga |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = 円山古墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom }} '''円山古墳'''（まるやまこふん）は、[[滋賀県]][[野洲市]]小篠原（こしのはら）にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[円墳]]。[[大岩山古墳群]]（国の[[史跡]]）を構成する古墳の1つ。 == 概要 ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+史跡「[[大岩山古墳群]]」8基 !地区 !古墳名 !colspan=2|墳丘 !築造時期 |- |rowspan=2|冨波||[[冨波古墳]]||[[前方後方墳]]||墳丘長42m||3c後半 |- |[[古冨波山古墳]]||[[円墳]]||直径30m||3c後半 |- |辻町||[[大塚山古墳 (野洲市)|大塚山古墳]]||[[帆立貝形古墳]]|| ||5c前半 |- |冨波||[[亀塚古墳 (野洲市)|亀塚古墳]]||帆立貝形古墳||墳丘長47m||5c後半-末 |- |rowspan=3|小篠原||[[天王山古墳 (野洲市)|天王山古墳]]||前方後円墳||墳丘長50m||6c初頭 |- |'''円山古墳'''||円墳||直径28m||6c前半 |- |[[甲山古墳 (野洲市)|甲山古墳]]||円墳||直径30m||6c中葉 |- |辻町||宮山2号墳||円墳||直径15m||6c末-7c前半 |}
滋賀県南部、[[野洲川]]右岸の大岩山から北に延びる丘陵の先端部に築造された古墳である。盗掘に遭っているほか、近年に史跡整備に伴う発掘調査が実施されている。 墳形は円形で、直径28メートル・高さ約8メートルを測る{{Sfn|円山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}{{Sfn|大岩山古墳群パンフレット}}（かつては[[帆立貝形古墳]]とする説もあった{{Sfn|滋賀県文化財学習シート|2004}}）。埋葬施設は片袖式の[[横穴式石室]]で、西方向に開口する{{Sfn|円山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。石室内には阿蘇溶結凝灰岩（阿蘇ピンク石/馬門石）製の刳抜式家形石棺（初葬棺）・[[二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)|二上山]]凝灰岩製の組合式家形石棺（追葬棺）の2基を据え、石室内からは銀製垂飾付耳飾・多量の鉄製武器類・1万点のガラス玉などの豊かな副葬品が出土している{{Sfn|円山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}{{Sfn|滋賀県文化財学習シート|2004}}。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]]後期の[[6世紀]]前半{{Sfn|大岩山古墳群パンフレット}}（または6世紀初頭{{Sfn|円山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}史跡説明板。）頃と推定される。大岩山古墳群8基のうちでは[[甲山古墳 (野洲市)|甲山古墳]]と前後する時期の首長墓（先行首長墓{{Sfn|大岩山古墳群パンフレット}}または後続首長墓{{Sfn|滋賀県文化財学習シート|2004}}）に位置づけられる。また野洲川流域において阿蘇溶結凝灰岩製石棺を最初に使用した古墳に位置づけられるとともに{{Sfn|大岩山古墳群パンフレット}}、石棺・副葬品の様相は甲山古墳と同様に被葬者の権力の大きさをうかがわせる古墳になる。 古墳域は[[1941年]]（[[昭和]]16年）に国の[[史跡]]に指定されている。現在は史跡整備の上で桜生（さくらばさま）史跡公園として公開されているが、石室内への立ち入りは制限され、毎年秋にのみ公開されている。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[1941年]]（[[昭和]]16年）12月13日、国の[[史跡]]に指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1579|大岩山古墳群}}。 * [[1982年]]（昭和57年）、測量調査{{Sfn|大岩山古墳群パンフレット}}。 * [[1985年]]（昭和60年）2月7日、既指定の史跡「円山古墳」・「甲山古墳」に従来未指定であった古墳6基を追加指定・統合して、史跡指定名称を「大岩山古墳群」に変更。 * 近年、史跡整備に伴う発掘調査（野洲町教育委員会、[[2001年]]に報告）。 * 2001年（[[平成]]13年）11月3日、桜生史跡公園の開園{{Sfn|大岩山古墳群パンフレット}}。 == 埋葬施設 == [[File:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|石室俯瞰図}}]] [[File:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|石室展開図}}]] 埋葬施設としては片袖式の[[横穴式石室]]が構築され、西方向に開口する{{Sfn|円山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。石室の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|円山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}{{Sfn|大岩山古墳群パンフレット}}。 * 石室全長：10.8メートル * 玄室：長さ4.3メートル、幅2.4メートル、高さ3.1メートル * 羨道：長さ6.5メートル、幅1.4メートル 羨道は入り口から玄室床面まで約1メートル下がり、天井石も階段状に下がる形態とする{{Sfn|円山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}{{Sfn|大岩山古墳群パンフレット}}。玄室の床面には玉石を敷き詰める{{Sfn|滋賀県文化財学習シート|2004}}。 玄室内には、刳抜式家形石棺・組合式家形石棺各1基を据える。それぞれの詳細は次の通り。 * 刳抜式家形石棺 *: 玄室手前寄りに据え、初葬棺と推定される。 *: 阿蘇溶結凝灰岩（[[阿蘇ピンク石]]/馬門石）製。長さ2.85メートル・幅1.46メートル・高さ1.83メートルを測る。蓋石には長辺各2個・短辺各1個の縄掛突起を有し、全体に赤色顔料を塗布する{{Sfn|円山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}{{Sfn|大岩山古墳群パンフレット}}。 * 組合式家形石棺 *: 玄室奥寄り、刳抜式家形石棺と奥壁の間に据え、追葬棺と推定される。 *: [[二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)|二上山]]凝灰岩製。蓋石・手前側石は破壊されている。底石は長さ2.45メートル・幅1.20メートル、棺身は高さ約0.80メートルを測る。長側石には長方形の突起を有する。滋賀県内の二上山凝灰岩製石棺としては[[鴨稲荷山古墳]]（[[高島市]]）とともに古式とされる{{Sfn|円山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}{{Sfn|大岩山古墳群パンフレット}}。 Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), genshitsu-2.jpg|玄室（奥壁方向） Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), sendou-2.jpg|羨道（開口部方向） Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), sendou-1.jpg|羨道（玄室方向） == 出土品 == 調査で検出された主な副葬品は次の通り{{Sfn|円山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 * 装身具 ** 銀製垂飾付耳飾 ** 銀製螺旋状耳飾 ** 歩揺 ** 銀製空玉 ** ガラス玉 約1万点{{Sfn|滋賀県文化財学習シート|2004}} * 武器類 ** 挂甲 ** 衝角付横矧板鋲留冑 ** 捩環頭大刀 ** 銀製捩金具 ** 鹿角製把小刀 ** 鉄鉾 10組以上 ** 鉄鏃 500本以上 ** 胡簶 * 馬具類 ** 鞍金具 ** 鐙 ** 轡 == 関連施設 == * 桜生史跡公園案内所（野洲市小篠原） * 野洲市歴史民俗博物館（銅鐸博物館）（野洲市辻町） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[https://www.yasu-kankou.com/common/files/pamph_07.pdf 大岩山古墳群パンフレット]}}（野洲市教育委員会文化財保護課 桜生史跡公園案内所（リンクは野洲市観光物産協会））|ref={{Harvid|大岩山古墳群パンフレット}}}} * 史跡説明板（旧野洲町教育委員会、2001年設置） * 地方自治体発行 ** {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[http://www.shiga-ec.ed.jp/www/contents/1438304524592/html/common/other/55d173d3025.pdf 「大岩山古墳群」]}} 滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課、2004年（滋賀県総合教育センター「滋賀県文化財学習シート」）|ref={{Harvid|滋賀県文化財学習シート|2004}}}}。 * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1991|chapter=大岩山古墳群|title=滋賀県の地名|series=[[日本歴史地名大系]]25|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490255|ref={{Harvid|大岩山古墳群（平凡社）|1991}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|author=大塚初重|authorlink=大塚初重|year=1989|chapter=円山古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|円山古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=杉本源造|year=2002|chapter=円山古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|円山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=大手前女子大学考古学研究会|editor-link=大手前女子大学|author=|year=1983|chapter=|title=史跡円山古墳|publisher=野洲町教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2001|chapter=|title=史跡大岩山古墳群 天王山古墳・円山古墳・甲山古墳調査整備報告書|series=野洲町文化財資料集2001-2|publisher=野洲町教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Maruyama Kofun (Yasu)}} * [[大岩山古墳群]] {{デフォルトソート:まるやまこふん やすし}} [[Category:滋賀県の古墳]] [[Category:野洲市の歴史]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:滋賀県にある国指定の史跡]]

高さ8m |埋葬施設 = 片袖式[[横穴式石室]]
（内部に家形石棺2基） |出土品 = 副葬品多数 |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]前半（または6世紀初頭） |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 国の[[史跡]]「大岩山古墳群」に包含 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Shiga |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = 円山古墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom }} '''円山古墳'''（まるやまこふん）は、[[滋賀県]][[野洲市]]小篠原（こしのはら）にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[円墳]]。[[大岩山古墳群]]（国の[[史跡]]）を構成する古墳の1つ。 == 概要 ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+史跡「[[大岩山古墳群]]」8基 !地区 !古墳名 !colspan=2|墳丘 !築造時期 |- |rowspan=2|冨波||[[冨波古墳]]||[[前方後方墳]]||墳丘長42m||3c後半 |- |[[古冨波山古墳]]||[[円墳]]||直径30m||3c後半 |- |辻町||[[大塚山古墳 (野洲市)|大塚山古墳]]||[[帆立貝形古墳]]|| ||5c前半 |- |冨波||[[亀塚古墳 (野洲市)|亀塚古墳]]||帆立貝形古墳||墳丘長47m||5c後半-末 |- |rowspan=3|小篠原||[[天王山古墳 (野洲市)|天王山古墳]]||前方後円墳||墳丘長50m||6c初頭 |- |'''円山古墳'''||円墳||直径28m||6c前半 |- |[[甲山古墳 (野洲市)|甲山古墳]]||円墳||直径30m||6c中葉 |- |辻町||宮山2号墳||円墳||直径15m||6c末-7c前半 |}

墳形は円形で、直径28メートル・高さ約8メートルを測る{{Sfn|円山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}{{Sfn|大岩山古墳群パンフレット}}（かつては[[帆立貝形古墳]]とする説もあった{{Sfn|滋賀県文化財学習シート|2004}}）。埋葬施設は片袖式の[[横穴式石室]]で、西方向に開口する{{Sfn|円山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。石室内には阿蘇溶結凝灰岩（阿蘇ピンク石/馬門石）製の刳抜式家形石棺（初葬棺）・[[二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)|二上山]]凝灰岩製の組合式家形石棺（追葬棺）の2基を据え、石室内からは銀製垂飾付耳飾・多量の鉄製武器類・1万点のガラス玉などの豊かな副葬品が出土している{{Sfn|円山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}{{Sfn|滋賀県文化財学習シート|2004}}。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]]後期の[[6世紀]]前半{{Sfn|大岩山古墳群パンフレット}}（または6世紀初頭{{Sfn|円山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}史跡説明板。）頃と推定される。大岩山古墳群8基のうちでは[[甲山古墳 (野洲市)|甲山古墳]]と前後する時期の首長墓（先行首長墓{{Sfn|大岩山古墳群パンフレット}}または後続首長墓{{Sfn|滋賀県文化財学習シート|2004}}）に位置づけられる。また野洲川流域において阿蘇溶結凝灰岩製石棺を最初に使用した古墳に位置づけられるとともに{{Sfn|大岩山古墳群パンフレット}}、石棺・副葬品の様相は甲山古墳と同様に被葬者の権力の大きさをうかがわせる古墳になる。 古墳域は[[1941年]]（[[昭和]]16年）に国の[[史跡]]に指定されている。現在は史跡整備の上で桜生（さくらばさま）史跡公園として公開されているが、石室内への立ち入りは制限され、毎年秋にのみ公開されている。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[1941年]]（[[昭和]]16年）12月13日、国の[[史跡]]に指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1579|大岩山古墳群}}。 * [[1982年]]（昭和57年）、測量調査{{Sfn|大岩山古墳群パンフレット}}。 * [[1985年]]（昭和60年）2月7日、既指定の史跡「円山古墳」・「甲山古墳」に従来未指定であった古墳6基を追加指定・統合して、史跡指定名称を「大岩山古墳群」に変更。 * 近年、史跡整備に伴う発掘調査（野洲町教育委員会、[[2001年]]に報告）。 * 2001年（[[平成]]13年）11月3日、桜生史跡公園の開園{{Sfn|大岩山古墳群パンフレット}}。 == 埋葬施設 == [[File:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|石室俯瞰図}}]] [[File:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|石室展開図}}]] 埋葬施設としては片袖式の[[横穴式石室]]が構築され、西方向に開口する{{Sfn|円山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。石室の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|円山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}{{Sfn|大岩山古墳群パンフレット}}。 * 石室全長：10.8メートル * 玄室：長さ4.3メートル、幅2.4メートル、高さ3.1メートル * 羨道：長さ6.5メートル、幅1.4メートル 羨道は入り口から玄室床面まで約1メートル下がり、天井石も階段状に下がる形態とする{{Sfn|円山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}{{Sfn|大岩山古墳群パンフレット}}。玄室の床面には玉石を敷き詰める{{Sfn|滋賀県文化財学習シート|2004}}。 玄室内には、刳抜式家形石棺・組合式家形石棺各1基を据える。それぞれの詳細は次の通り。 * 刳抜式家形石棺 *: 玄室手前寄りに据え、初葬棺と推定される。 *: 阿蘇溶結凝灰岩（[[阿蘇ピンク石]]/馬門石）製。長さ2.85メートル・幅1.46メートル・高さ1.83メートルを測る。蓋石には長辺各2個・短辺各1個の縄掛突起を有し、全体に赤色顔料を塗布する{{Sfn|円山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}{{Sfn|大岩山古墳群パンフレット}}。 * 組合式家形石棺 *: 玄室奥寄り、刳抜式家形石棺と奥壁の間に据え、追葬棺と推定される。 *: [[二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)|二上山]]凝灰岩製。蓋石・手前側石は破壊されている。底石は長さ2.45メートル・幅1.20メートル、棺身は高さ約0.80メートルを測る。長側石には長方形の突起を有する。滋賀県内の二上山凝灰岩製石棺としては[[鴨稲荷山古墳]]（[[高島市]]）とともに古式とされる{{Sfn|円山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}{{Sfn|大岩山古墳群パンフレット}}。 Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), genshitsu-2.jpg|玄室（奥壁方向） Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), sendou-2.jpg|羨道（開口部方向） Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), sendou-1.jpg|羨道（玄室方向） == 出土品 == 調査で検出された主な副葬品は次の通り{{Sfn|円山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 * 装身具 ** 銀製垂飾付耳飾 ** 銀製螺旋状耳飾 ** 歩揺 ** 銀製空玉 ** ガラス玉 約1万点{{Sfn|滋賀県文化財学習シート|2004}} * 武器類 ** 挂甲 ** 衝角付横矧板鋲留冑 ** 捩環頭大刀 ** 銀製捩金具 ** 鹿角製把小刀 ** 鉄鉾 10組以上 ** 鉄鏃 500本以上 ** 胡簶 * 馬具類 ** 鞍金具 ** 鐙 ** 轡 == 関連施設 == * 桜生史跡公園案内所（野洲市小篠原） * 野洲市歴史民俗博物館（銅鐸博物館）（野洲市辻町） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[https://www.yasu-kankou.com/common/files/pamph_07.pdf 大岩山古墳群パンフレット]}}（野洲市教育委員会文化財保護課 桜生史跡公園案内所（リンクは野洲市観光物産協会））|ref={{Harvid|大岩山古墳群パンフレット}}}} * 史跡説明板（旧野洲町教育委員会、2001年設置） * 地方自治体発行 ** Mound and stone chamber entrance |Other_name= | part_of={{ill|Oiwayama Kofun Group|ja|大岩山古墳群}} |location={{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}{{ill|Yasu|ja|野洲市}} Koshinohara (Azamaruyama)
(Sakuraba Historic Park) |Lat_deg=35|Lat_min=4|Lat_sec=26.82 |Lon_deg=136|Lon_min=2|Lon_sec=28.35 |omitted=JP-25 |type={{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} |map_size=Diameter 28m
Height 8m |burial=One-sided {{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}
(Two house-shaped sarcophagi inside) |burial goods=Numerous grave goods |Accompanying_mounds= |built=Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} (or early 6th century) |Interred_individual= |Mausoleum= |Cultural_property=Included in the national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} "Oiwayama Kofun Group" |Tangible_notes= |Special_notes= |map=Japan Shiga |type=円墳 |map_dot_label=Maruyama Kofun |map_dot_label=bottom }} '''Maruyama Kofun''' (円山古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Yasu|ja|野洲市}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}, in the Koshinohara area. It has a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} type and is one of the Kofuns that comprise the {{ill|Ōiwayama Kofun Cluster|ja|大岩山古墳群}}, Historic site as a national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}. == Overview == This kofun was constructed at the northern tip of a hill extending from Oiwayama to the right bank of the Yasu River in southern Shiga Prefecture. While the mound has been subject to looting, recent excavations were conducted as part of historic site maintenance efforts. The mound is circular, measuring 28 meters in diameter and approximately 8 meters in height {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}{{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. Initially thought to be a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}}, it is now recognized as a round kofun {{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture designation1 Learning Sheet|2004}}. The burial facility consists of a one-sided {{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening westward {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Inside the chamber are two house-shaped sarcophagi: one made of Aso welded tuff (Aso pink stone/Makado stone) and another made of {{ill|Futagoyama|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations}} tuff. These sarcophagi, along with a variety of grave goods such as silver ear ornaments, iron weapons, and over 10,000 glass beads, were uncovered during excavations {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}{{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture designation1 Learning Sheet|2004}}. The construction period is estimated to be the early 6th century, placing it in the late Kofun period {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. It is believed to be the tomb of a regional leader and represents the first use of Aso welded tuff sarcophagi in the Yasu River basin {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. The burial goods reflect the power and influence of the entombed individual. The kofun site was Historic site a national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} in 1941. It has since been preserved and opened to the public as part of the Sakuraba Historic Park, though access to the stone chamber is restricted and allowed only during annual autumn openings. == Archaeological History == * '''1941''': Designated as a national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} (December 13) {{National Historic site Database|401|1579|Oiwayama Kofun Group}}. * '''1982''': Surveyed {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. * '''1985''': Six additional mounds were incorporated into the previously Historic site historic sites "Maruyama Kofun" and "Kozan Kofun," leading to the renaming of the Historic site as "Oiwayama Kofun Group" . * '''2001''': Excavations and maintenance reports by the Yasu Town Board of Education. * '''2001''': Sakuraba Historic Park opened on November 3 {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. == burial == [[File:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the burial chamber}}]] [[File:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the burial chamber}}]] The burial facility is a one-sided {{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the west {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Its area are as follows {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}{{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}: * Total length: 10.8 meters * Chamber: Length 4.3 meters, width 2.4 meters, height 3.1 meters * Passage: Length 6.5 meters, width 1.4 meters The passage descends approximately 1 meter from the entrance to the chamber floor, with the ceiling stones also stepping down {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The chamber floor is paved with pebbles {{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture designation1 Learning Sheet|2004}}. Inside the chamber are two sarcophagi: * '''Hollowed-out house-shaped sarcophagus''': Located near the front of the chamber and presumed to be the primary burial. Made of Aso welded tuff (Aso pink stone/Makado stone), it measures 2.85 meters in length, 1.46 meters in width, and 1.83 meters in height. The lid stone has rope-loop projections and is painted with red pigment {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. * '''Assembled house-shaped sarcophagus''': Positioned at the back of the chamber, made of Futagoyama tuff. Its lid and front stones are damaged, but the base stone measures 2.45 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width, while the sarcophagus body is about 0.8 meters in height. This is considered one of the oldest examples of Futagoyama tuff sarcophagi in Shiga Prefecture {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), genshitsu-2.jpg|Chamber (facing rear wall) Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), sendou-2.jpg|Passage (facing entrance) Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), sendou-1.jpg|Passage (facing chamber) == burial goods == Notable grave goods recovered include {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}: * '''Ornaments''' ** Silver ear ornaments with pendants ** Silver spiral earrings ** Step pendants ** Silver hollow beads ** ~10,000 glass beads {{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture designation1 Learning Sheet|2004}} * '''Weapons''' ** Lamellar armor ** Helmet with horizontal riveting ** Sword with twisted ring pommel ** Silver spiral fittings ** Small knife with antler handle ** Over 10 iron spearheads ** Over 500 iron arrowheads ** Quiver * '''Horse gear''' ** Saddle fittings ** Stirrups ** Bits == Related Facilities == * Sakuraba Historic Park Intypeation Center (Yasu City, Koshinohara) * Yasu City Museum of History and Folklore (Dotaku Museum, Yasu City, Tsuji Town) == References == Height 8m |burial=One-sided {{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}
(Two house-shaped sarcophagi inside) |burial goods=Numerous grave goods |Accompanying_mounds= |built=Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft}} (or early 6th century) |Interred_individual= |Mausoleum= |Cultural_property=Included in the national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft}}} "Oiwayama Kofun Group" |Tangible_notes= |Special_notes= |map=Japan Shiga |type=円墳 |map_dot_label=Maruyama Kofun |map_dot_label=bottom }} '''Maruyama Kofun''' (円山古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Yasu|ja|野洲市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|12=draft}}, in the Koshinohara area. It has a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} type and is one of the Kofuns that comprise the {{ill|Ōiwayama Kofun Cluster|ja|大岩山古墳群|12=draft}}, Historic site as a national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft}}}. == Overview == This kofun was constructed at the northern tip of a hill extending from Oiwayama to the right bank of the Yasu River in southern Shiga Prefecture. While the mound has been subject to looting, recent excavations were conducted as part of historic site maintenance efforts. The mound is circular, measuring 28 meters in diameter and approximately 8 meters in height {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}{{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. Initially thought to be a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft}}, it is now recognized as a round kofun {{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture designation1 Learning Sheet|2004}}. The burial facility consists of a one-sided {{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} opening westward {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Inside the chamber are two house-shaped sarcophagi: one made of Aso welded tuff (Aso pink stone/Makado stone) and another made of {{ill|Futagoyama|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|12=draft}} tuff. These sarcophagi, along with a variety of grave goods such as silver ear ornaments, iron weapons, and over 10,000 glass beads, were uncovered during excavations {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}{{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture designation1 Learning Sheet|2004}}. The construction period is estimated to be the early 6th century, placing it in the late Kofun period {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. It is believed to be the tomb of a regional leader and represents the first use of Aso welded tuff sarcophagi in the Yasu River basin {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. The burial goods reflect the power and influence of the entombed individual. The kofun site was Historic site a national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft}}} in 1941. It has since been preserved and opened to the public as part of the Sakuraba Historic Park, though access to the stone chamber is restricted and allowed only during annual autumn openings. == Archaeological History == * '''1941''': Designated as a national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft}}} (December 13) {{National Historic site Database|401|1579|Oiwayama Kofun Group}}. * '''1982''': Surveyed {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. * '''1985''': Six additional mounds were incorporated into the previously Historic site historic sites "Maruyama Kofun" and "Kozan Kofun," leading to the renaming of the Historic site as "Oiwayama Kofun Group" . * '''2001''': Excavations and maintenance reports by the Yasu Town Board of Education. * '''2001''': Sakuraba Historic Park opened on November 3 {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. == burial == [[File:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the burial chamber}}]] [[File:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the burial chamber}}]] The burial facility is a one-sided {{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} opening to the west {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Its area are as follows {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}{{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}: * Total length: 10.8 meters * Chamber: Length 4.3 meters, width 2.4 meters, height 3.1 meters * Passage: Length 6.5 meters, width 1.4 meters The passage descends approximately 1 meter from the entrance to the chamber floor, with the ceiling stones also stepping down {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The chamber floor is paved with pebbles {{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture designation1 Learning Sheet|2004}}. Inside the chamber are two sarcophagi: * '''Hollowed-out house-shaped sarcophagus''': Located near the front of the chamber and presumed to be the primary burial. Made of Aso welded tuff (Aso pink stone/Makado stone), it measures 2.85 meters in length, 1.46 meters in width, and 1.83 meters in height. The lid stone has rope-loop projections and is painted with red pigment {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. * '''Assembled house-shaped sarcophagus''': Positioned at the back of the chamber, made of Futagoyama tuff. Its lid and front stones are damaged, but the base stone measures 2.45 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width, while the sarcophagus body is about 0.8 meters in height. This is considered one of the oldest examples of Futagoyama tuff sarcophagi in Shiga Prefecture {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), genshitsu-2.jpg|Chamber (facing rear wall) Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), sendou-2.jpg|Passage (facing entrance) Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), sendou-1.jpg|Passage (facing chamber) == burial goods == Notable grave goods recovered include {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}: * '''Ornaments''' ** Silver ear ornaments with pendants ** Silver spiral earrings ** Step pendants ** Silver hollow beads ** ~10,000 glass beads {{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture designation1 Learning Sheet|2004}} * '''Weapons''' ** Lamellar armor ** Helmet with horizontal riveting ** Sword with twisted ring pommel ** Silver spiral fittings ** Small knife with antler handle ** Over 10 iron spearheads ** Over 500 iron arrowheads ** Quiver * '''Horse gear''' ** Saddle fittings ** Stirrups ** Height 8m |burial=One-sided {{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}
(Two house-shaped sarcophagi inside) |burial goods=Numerous grave goods |Accompanying_mounds= |built=Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft|12=draft}} (or early 6th century) |Interred_individual= |Mausoleum= |Cultural_property=Included in the national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft|12=draft}}} "Oiwayama Kofun Group" |Tangible_notes= |Special_notes= |map=Japan Shiga |type=円墳 |map_dot_label=Maruyama Kofun |map_dot_label=bottom }} '''Maruyama Kofun''' (円山古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Yasu|ja|野洲市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|12=draft|12=draft}}, in the Koshinohara area. It has a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} type and is one of the Kofuns that comprise the {{ill|Ōiwayama Kofun Cluster|ja|大岩山古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}, Historic site as a national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft|12=draft}}}. == Overview == This kofun was constructed at the northern tip of a hill extending from Oiwayama to the right bank of the Yasu River in southern Shiga Prefecture. While the mound has been subject to looting, recent excavations were conducted as part of historic site maintenance efforts. The mound is circular, measuring 28 meters in diameter and approximately 8 meters in height {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}{{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. Initially thought to be a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}, it is now recognized as a round kofun {{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture designation1 Learning Sheet|2004}}. The burial facility consists of a one-sided {{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} opening westward {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Inside the chamber are two house-shaped sarcophagi: one made of Aso welded tuff (Aso pink stone/Makado stone) and another made of {{ill|Futagoyama|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|12=draft|12=draft}} tuff. These sarcophagi, along with a variety of grave goods such as silver ear ornaments, iron weapons, and over 10,000 glass beads, were uncovered during excavations {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}{{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture designation1 Learning Sheet|2004}}. The construction period is estimated to be the early 6th century, placing it in the late Kofun period {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. It is believed to be the tomb of a regional leader and represents the first use of Aso welded tuff sarcophagi in the Yasu River basin {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. The burial goods reflect the power and influence of the entombed individual. The kofun site was Historic site a national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft|12=draft}}} in 1941. It has since been preserved and opened to the public as part of the Sakuraba Historic Park, though access to the stone chamber is restricted and allowed only during annual autumn openings. == Archaeological History == * '''1941''': Designated as a national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft|12=draft}}} (December 13) {{National Historic site Database|401|1579|Oiwayama Kofun Group}}. * '''1982''': Surveyed {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. * '''1985''': Six additional mounds were incorporated into the previously Historic site historic sites "Maruyama Kofun" and "Kozan Kofun," leading to the renaming of the Historic site as "Oiwayama Kofun Group" . * '''2001''': Excavations and maintenance reports by the Yasu Town Board of Education. * '''2001''': Sakuraba Historic Park opened on November 3 {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. == burial == [[File:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the burial chamber}}]] [[File:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the burial chamber}}]] The burial facility is a one-sided {{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} opening to the west {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Its area are as follows {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}{{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}: * Total length: 10.8 meters * Chamber: Length 4.3 meters, width 2.4 meters, height 3.1 meters * Passage: Length 6.5 meters, width 1.4 meters The passage descends approximately 1 meter from the entrance to the chamber floor, with the ceiling stones also stepping down {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The chamber floor is paved with pebbles {{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture designation1 Learning Sheet|2004}}. Inside the chamber are two sarcophagi: * '''Hollowed-out house-shaped sarcophagus''': Located near the front of the chamber and presumed to be the primary burial. Made of Aso welded tuff (Aso pink stone/Makado stone), it measures 2.85 meters in length, 1.46 meters in width, and 1.83 meters in height. The lid stone has rope-loop projections and is painted with red pigment {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. * '''Assembled house-shaped sarcophagus''': Positioned at the back of the chamber, made of Futagoyama tuff. Its lid and front stones are damaged, but the base stone measures 2.45 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width, while the sarcophagus body is about 0.8 meters in height. This is considered one of the oldest examples of Futagoyama tuff sarcophagi in Shiga Prefecture {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), genshitsu-2.jpg|Chamber (facing rear wall) Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), sendou-2.jpg|Passage (facing entrance) Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), sendou-1.jpg|Passage (facing chamber) == burial goods == Notable grave goods recovered include {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}: * '''Ornaments''' ** Silver ear ornaments with pendants ** Silver spiral earrings ** Step pendants ** Silver hollow beads ** ~10,000 glass beads {{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture designation1 Learning Sheet|2004}} * '''Weapons''' ** Lamellar armor ** Helmet with horizontal riveting ** Sword with twisted ring pommel ** Silver spiral fittings ** Small knife with antler handle ** Over 10 iron spearheads ** Over 500 iron arrowheads ** Quiver * '''Horse gear''' ** Saddle fittings ** Stirrups ** Bits == Related Facilities == * Sakuraba Historic Park Intypeation Center (Yasu City, Koshinohara) * Yasu City Museum of History and Folklore (Dotaku Museum, Yasu City, Tsuji Town) == References == This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |name=Maruyama Kofun |image=[[File:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), entrance.jpg|280px]]
Mound and stone chamber entrance |Other_name= | part_of={{ill|Oiwayama Kofun Group|ja|大岩山古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |location={{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Yasu|ja|野洲市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Koshinohara (Azamaruyama)
(Sakuraba Historic Park) |Lat_deg=35|Lat_min=4|Lat_sec=26.82 |Lon_deg=136|Lon_min=2|Lon_sec=28.35 |omitted=JP-25 |type={{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |map_size=Diameter 28m
Height 8m |burial=One-sided {{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
(Two house-shaped sarcophagi inside) |burial goods=Numerous grave goods |Accompanying_mounds= |built=Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (or early 6th century) |Interred_individual= |Mausoleum= |Cultural_property=Included in the national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|qq=draft|qq=draft}}} "Oiwayama Kofun Group" |Tangible_notes= |Special_notes= |map=Japan Shiga |type=円墳 |map_dot_label=Maruyama Kofun |map_dot_label=bottom }} '''Maruyama Kofun''' (円山古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Yasu|ja|野洲市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, in the Koshinohara area. It has a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} type and is one of the Kofuns that comprise the {{ill|Ōiwayama Kofun Cluster|ja|大岩山古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Historic site as a national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}. == Overview == This kofun was constructed at the northern tip of a hill extending from Oiwayama to the right bank of the Yasu River in southern Shiga Prefecture. While the mound has been subject to looting, recent excavations were conducted as part of historic site maintenance efforts. The mound is circular, measuring 28 meters in diameter and approximately 8 meters in height {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}{{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. Initially thought to be a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it is now recognized as a round kofun {{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture designation1 Learning Sheet|2004}}. The burial facility consists of a one-sided {{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} opening westward {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Inside the chamber are two house-shaped sarcophagi: one made of Aso welded tuff (Aso pink stone/Makado stone) and another made of {{ill|Futagoyama|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|qq=draft|qq=draft}} tuff. These sarcophagi, along with a variety of grave goods such as silver ear ornaments, iron weapons, and over 10,000 glass beads, were uncovered during excavations {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}{{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture designation1 Learning Sheet|2004}}. The construction period is estimated to be the early 6th century, placing it in the late Kofun period {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. It is believed to be the tomb of a regional leader and represents the first use of Aso welded tuff sarcophagi in the Yasu River basin {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. The burial goods reflect the power and influence of the entombed individual. The kofun site was Historic site a national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|qq=draft|qq=draft}}} in 1941. It has since been preserved and opened to the public as part of the Sakuraba Historic Park, though access to the stone chamber is restricted and allowed only during annual autumn openings. == Archaeological History == * '''1941''': Designated as a national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|qq=draft|qq=draft}}} (December 13) {{National Historic site Database|401|1579|Oiwayama Kofun Group}}. * '''1982''': Surveyed {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. * '''1985''': Six additional mounds were incorporated into the previously Historic site historic sites "Maruyama Kofun" and "Kozan Kofun," leading to the renaming of the Historic site as "Oiwayama Kofun Group" . * '''2001''': Excavations and maintenance reports by the Yasu Town Board of Education. * '''2001''': Sakuraba Historic Park opened on November 3 {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. == burial == [[File:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the burial chamber}}]] [[File:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the burial chamber}}]] The burial facility is a one-sided {{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} opening to the west {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Its area are as follows {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}{{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}: * Total length: 10.8 meters * Chamber: Length 4.3 meters, width 2.4 meters, height 3.1 meters * Passage: Length 6.5 meters, width 1.4 meters The passage descends approximately 1 meter from the entrance to the chamber floor, with the ceiling stones also stepping down {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The chamber floor is paved with pebbles {{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture designation1 Learning Sheet|2004}}. Inside the chamber are two sarcophagi: * '''Hollowed-out house-shaped sarcophagus''': Located near the front of the chamber and presumed to be the primary burial. Made of Aso welded tuff (Aso pink stone/Makado stone), it measures 2.85 meters in length, 1.46 meters in width, and 1.83 meters in height. The lid stone has rope-loop projections and is painted with red pigment {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. * '''Assembled house-shaped sarcophagus''': Positioned at the back of the chamber, made of Futagoyama tuff. Its lid and front stones are damaged, but the base stone measures 2.45 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width, while the sarcophagus body is about 0.8 meters in height. This is considered one of the oldest examples of Futagoyama tuff sarcophagi in Shiga Prefecture {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), genshitsu-2.jpg|Chamber (facing rear wall) Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), sendou-2.jpg|Passage (facing entrance) Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), sendou-1.jpg|Passage (facing chamber) == burial goods == Notable grave goods recovered include {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}: * '''Ornaments''' ** Silver ear ornaments with pendants ** Silver spiral earrings ** Step pendants ** Silver hollow beads ** ~10,000 glass beads {{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture designation1 Learning Sheet|2004}} * '''Weapons''' ** Lamellar armor ** Helmet with horizontal riveting ** Sword with twisted ring pommel ** Silver spiral fittings ** Small knife with antler handle ** Over 10 iron spearheads ** Over 500 iron arrowheads ** Quiver * '''Horse gear''' ** Saddle fittings ** Stirrups ** Bits == Related Facilities == * Sakuraba Historic Park Intypeation Center (Yasu City, Koshinohara) * Yasu City Museum of History and Folklore (Dotaku Museum, Yasu City, Tsuji Town) == References == Sakuraba Historic Park Intypeation Center, linked from Yasu City Tourism Association) {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. * Explanation boards (typeer Yasu Town Board of Education, 2001). * designation1 Protection Section, 2004. * Encyclopedias: ** {{Cite book|title=Maruyama Kofun|series=Continued Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Shuppan|year=2002}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōiwayama It has a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} type and is one of the Kofuns that comprise the {{ill|Ōiwayama Kofun Cluster|ja|大岩山古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōiwayama Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, Historic site as a national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic site|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic site|qq=}}}. == Overview == This kofun was constructed at the northern tip of a hill extending from Oiwayama to the right bank of the Yasu River in southern Shiga Prefecture. While the mound has been subject to looting, recent excavations were conducted as part of historic site maintenance efforts. The mound is circular, measuring 28 meters in diameter and approximately 8 meters in height {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}{{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. Initially thought to be a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=}}, it is now recognized as a round kofun {{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture designation1 Learning Sheet|2004}}. The burial facility consists of a one-sided {{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening westward {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Inside the chamber are two house-shaped sarcophagi: one made of Aso welded tuff (Aso pink stone/Makado stone) and another made of {{ill|Futagoyama|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futagoyama|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}} tuff. These sarcophagi, along with a variety of grave goods such as silver ear ornaments, iron weapons, and over 10,000 glass beads, were uncovered during excavations {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}{{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture designation1 Learning Sheet|2004}}. The construction period is estimated to be the early 6th century, placing it in the late Kofun period {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. It is believed to be the tomb of a regional leader and represents the first use of Aso welded tuff sarcophagi in the Yasu River basin {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. The burial goods reflect the power and influence of the entombed individual. The kofun site was Historic site a national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic site|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic site|qq=}}} in 1941. It has since been preserved and opened to the public as part of the Sakuraba Historic Park, though access to the stone chamber is restricted and allowed only during annual autumn openings. == Archaeological History == * '''1941''': Designated as a national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic site|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic site|qq=}}} (December 13) {{National Historic site Database|401|1579|Oiwayama Kofun Group}}. * '''1982''': Surveyed {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. * '''1985''': Six additional mounds were incorporated into the previously Historic site historic sites "Maruyama Kofun" and "Kozan Kofun," leading to the renaming of the Historic site as "Oiwayama Kofun Group" . * '''2001''': Excavations and maintenance reports by the Yasu Town Board of Education. * '''2001''': Sakuraba Historic Park opened on November 3 {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. == burial == [[File:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the burial chamber}}]] [[File:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the burial chamber}}]] The burial facility is a one-sided {{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the west {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Its area are as follows {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}{{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}: * Total length: 10.8 meters * Chamber: Length 4.3 meters, width 2.4 meters, height 3.1 meters * Passage: Length 6.5 meters, width 1.4 meters The passage descends approximately 1 meter from the entrance to the chamber floor, with the ceiling stones also stepping down {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The chamber floor is paved with pebbles {{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture designation1 Learning Sheet|2004}}. Inside the chamber are two sarcophagi: * '''Hollowed-out house-shaped sarcophagus''': Located near the front of the chamber and presumed to be the primary burial. Made of Aso welded tuff (Aso pink stone/Makado stone), it measures 2.85 meters in length, 1.46 meters in width, and 1.83 meters in height. The lid stone has rope-loop projections and is painted with red pigment {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. * '''Assembled house-shaped sarcophagus''': Positioned at the back of the chamber, made of Futagoyama tuff. Its lid and front stones are damaged, but the base stone measures 2.45 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width, while the sarcophagus body is about 0.8 meters in height. This is considered one of the oldest examples of Futagoyama tuff sarcophagi in Shiga Prefecture {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} |name=Maruyama Kofun |image=[[File:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), entrance.jpg|280px]]
Mound and stone chamber entrance |Other_name= | part_of={{ill|Oiwayama Kofun Group|ja|大岩山古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oiwayama Kofun Group|qq=}} |location={{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiga Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Yasu|ja|野洲市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasu|qq=}} Koshinohara (Azamaruyama)
(Sakuraba Historic Park) |Lat_deg=35|Lat_min=4|Lat_sec=26.82 |Lon_deg=136|Lon_min=2|Lon_sec=28.35 |omitted=JP-25 |type={{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} |map_size=Diameter 28m
Height 8m |burial=One-sided {{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}
(Two house-shaped sarcophagi inside) |burial goods=Numerous grave goods |Accompanying_mounds= |built=Early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/6th century|qq=}} (or early 6th century) |Interred_individual= |Mausoleum= |Cultural_property=Included in the national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic site|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic site|qq=}}} "Oiwayama Kofun Group" |Tangible_notes= |Special_notes= |map=Japan Shiga |type=円墳 |map_dot_label=Maruyama Kofun |map_dot_label=bottom }} '''Maruyama Kofun''' (円山古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Yasu|ja|野洲市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasu|qq=}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiga Prefecture|qq=}}, in the Koshinohara area. It has a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} type and is one of the Kofuns that comprise the {{ill|Ōiwayama Kofun Cluster|ja|大岩山古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōiwayama Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, Historic site as a national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic site|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic site|qq=}}}. == Overview == This kofun was constructed at the northern tip of a hill extending from Oiwayama to the right bank of the Yasu River in southern Shiga Prefecture. While the mound has been subject to looting, recent excavations were conducted as part of historic site maintenance efforts. The mound is circular, measuring 28 meters in diameter and approximately 8 meters in height {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}{{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. Initially thought to be a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=}}, it is now recognized as a round kofun {{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture designation1 Learning Sheet|2004}}. The burial facility consists of a one-sided {{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening westward {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Inside the chamber are two house-shaped sarcophagi: one made of Aso welded tuff (Aso pink stone/Makado stone) and another made of {{ill|Futagoyama|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futagoyama|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}} tuff. These sarcophagi, along with a variety of grave goods such as silver ear ornaments, iron weapons, and over 10,000 glass beads, were uncovered during excavations {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}{{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture designation1 Learning Sheet|2004}}. The construction period is estimated to be the early 6th century, placing it in the late Kofun period {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. It is believed to be the tomb of a regional leader and represents the first use of Aso welded tuff sarcophagi in the Yasu River basin {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. The burial goods reflect the power and influence of the entombed individual. The kofun site was Historic site a national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic site|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic site|qq=}}} in 1941. It has since been preserved and opened to the public as part of the Sakuraba Historic Park, though access to the stone chamber is restricted and allowed only during annual autumn openings. == Archaeological History == * '''1941''': Designated as a national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic site|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic site|qq=}}} (December 13) {{National Historic site Database|401|1579|Oiwayama Kofun Group}}. * '''1982''': Surveyed {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. * '''1985''': Six additional mounds were incorporated into the previously Historic site historic sites "Maruyama Kofun" and "Kozan Kofun," leading to the renaming of the Historic site as "Oiwayama Kofun Group" . * '''2001''': Excavations and maintenance reports by the Yasu Town Board of Education. * '''2001''': Sakuraba Historic Park opened on November 3 {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. == burial == [[File:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the burial chamber}}]] [[File:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the burial chamber}}]] The burial facility is a one-sided {{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the west {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Its area are as follows {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}{{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}: * Total length: 10.8 meters * Chamber: Length 4.3 meters, width 2.4 meters, height 3.1 meters * Passage: Length 6.5 meters, width 1.4 meters The passage descends approximately 1 meter from the entrance to the chamber floor, with the ceiling stones also stepping down {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The chamber floor is paved with pebbles {{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture designation1 Learning Sheet|2004}}. Inside the chamber are two sarcophagi: * '''Hollowed-out house-shaped sarcophagus''': Located near the front of the chamber and presumed to be the primary burial. Made of Aso welded tuff (Aso pink stone/Makado stone), it measures 2.85 meters in length, 1.46 meters in width, and 1.83 meters in height. The lid stone has rope-loop projections and is painted with red pigment {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. * '''Assembled house-shaped sarcophagus''': Positioned at the back of the chamber, made of Futagoyama tuff. Its lid and front stones are damaged, but the base stone measures 2.45 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width, while the sarcophagus body is about 0.8 meters in height. This is considered one of the oldest examples of Futagoyama tuff sarcophagi in Shiga Prefecture {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), genshitsu-2.jpg|Chamber (facing rear wall) Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), sendou-2.jpg|Passage (facing entrance) Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), sendou-1.jpg|Passage (facing chamber) == burial goods == Notable grave goods recovered include {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}: * '''Ornaments''' ** Silver ear ornaments with pendants ** Silver spiral earrings ** Step pendants ** Silver hollow beads ** ~10,000 glass beads {{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture designation1 Learning Sheet|2004}} * '''Weapons''' ** Lamellar armor ** Helmet with horizontal riveting ** Sword with twisted ring pommel ** Silver spiral fittings ** Small knife with antler handle ** Over 10 iron spearheads ** Over 500 iron arrowheads ** Quiver * '''Horse gear''' ** Saddle fittings ** Stirrups ** Bits == Related Facilities == * Sakuraba Historic Park Intypeation Center (Yasu City, Koshinohara) * Yasu City Museum of History and Folklore (Dotaku Museum, Yasu City, Tsuji Town) == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{PDFlink|[https://www.yasu-kankou.com/common/files/pamph_07.pdf Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet]}} (Yasu City Board of Education, designation1 Protection Section, Sakuraba Historic Park Intypeation Center, linked from Yasu City Tourism Association) {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. * Explanation boards (typeer Yasu Town Board of Education, 2001). * Prefectural publications: ** {{PDFlink|[http://www.shiga-ec.ed.jp/www/contents/1438304524592/html/common/other/55d173d3025.pdf "Oiwayama Kofun Group"]}} Shiga Prefectural Board of Education, designation1 Protection Section, 2004. * Encyclopedias: ** {{Cite book|title=Maruyama Kofun|series=Continued Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Shuppan|year=2002}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōiwayama Kofun Cluster|ja|大岩山古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōiwayama Kofun Cluster|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|円山古墳 (野洲市)|version=101832160|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yasu]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:National Historic sites of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] }} [[sn:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu)]] [[ja:円山古墳 (野洲市)]] (Two house-shaped sarcophagi inside) |burial goods=Numerous grave goods |Accompanying_mounds= |built=Early [[:en:6th century|6th century]] (or early 6th century) |Interred_individual= |Mausoleum= |Cultural_property=Included in the national [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} "Oiwayama Kofun Group" |Tangible_notes= |Special_notes= |map=Japan Shiga |type=円墳 |map_dot_label=Maruyama Kofun |map_dot_label=bottom }} '''Maruyama Kofun''' (円山古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Yasu, Shiga|Yasu]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], in the Koshinohara area. While the mound has been subject to looting, recent excavations were conducted as part of historic site maintenance efforts. It has since been preserved and opened to the public as part of the Sakuraba Historic Park, though access to the stone chamber is restricted and allowed only during annual autumn openings. == Archaeological History == * '''1941''': Designated as a national [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} (December 13) {{National Historic site Database|401|1579|Oiwayama Kofun Group}}. * '''1982''': Surveyed {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. * '''1985''': Six additional mounds were incorporated into the previously Historic site historic sites "Maruyama Kofun" and "Kozan Kofun," leading to the renaming of the Historic site as "Oiwayama Kofun Group" . * '''2001''': Excavations and maintenance reports by the Yasu Town Board of Education. * '''2001''': Sakuraba Historic Park opened on November 3 {{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}. == burial == [[File:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the burial chamber}}]] [[File:Maruyama Kofun (Yasu), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the burial chamber}}]] The burial facility is a one-sided {{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the west {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. Its area are as follows {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}{{Sfn|Oiwayama Kofun Group Pamphlet}}: * Total length: 10.8 meters * Chamber: Length 4.3 meters, width 2.4 meters, height 3.1 meters * Passage: Length 6.5 meters, width 1.4 meters The passage descends approximately 1 meter from the entrance to the chamber floor, with the ceiling stones also stepping down {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. The chamber floor is paved with pebbles {{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture designation1 Learning Sheet|2004}}. Inside the chamber are two sarcophagi: * '''Hollowed-out house-shaped sarcophagus''': Located near the front of the chamber and presumed to be the primary burial. Made of Aso welded tuff (Aso pink stone/Makado stone), it measures 2.85 meters in length, 1.46 meters in width, and 1.83 meters in height. The lid stone has rope-loop projections and is painted with red pigment {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. * '''Assembled house-shaped sarcophagus''': Positioned at the back of the chamber, made of Futagoyama tuff. Its lid and front stones are damaged, but the base stone measures 2.45 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width, while the sarcophagus body is about 0.8 meters in height. This is considered one of the oldest examples of Futagoyama tuff sarcophagi in Shiga Prefecture {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}. goods == Notable grave goods recovered include {{Sfn|Maruyama Kofun (Continued Kofun Encyclopedia)|2002}}: * '''Ornaments''' ** Silver ear ornaments with pendants ** Silver spiral earrings ** Step pendants ** Silver hollow beads ** ~10,000 glass beads {{Sfn|Shiga Prefecture designation1 Learning Sheet|2004}} * '''Weapons''' ** Lamellar armor ** Helmet with horizontal riveting ** Sword with
